## Phased Implementation Plan

**Platform order:** aarch64 first, x86_64 second.

### Phase 0: Architecture Documentation (complete)

Full documentation and project scaffolding so future contributors have complete context.

**Deliverables:** `docs/` directory with architecture, IPC, syscall catalog, servers, boot, drivers, musl, memory layout, build, and MINIX 3 mapping documentation. Cargo workspace with all crate placeholders. CLAUDE.md, LICENSE (BSD-3-Clause).

**Milestone:** `docs/` is comprehensive enough to start coding Phase 1 without re-exploring MINIX 3.

### Phase 1: Kernel Scaffolding + Boot (aarch64, QEMU virt) (complete)

- Cargo workspace with `kernel`, `kernel-shared` crates
- Builds via `aarch64-unknown-none` + linker script (`kernel/src/arch/aarch64/linker.ld`); the bespoke `aarch64-minix-kernel.json` target spec is deferred to Phase 2 (the stock target is sufficient for boot)
- Vendor Limine v9.x binary + header (`external/limine/Makefile`); Rust-side request block in `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/limine.rs` (base revision, HHDM, memmap, paging mode, stack size)
- PL011 UART driver at `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/uart.rs` with `core::fmt::Write` adapter; MMIO base is HHDM-relative (Limine base revision 2 keeps the [0, 4 GiB) blanket map covering PL011)
- aarch64 exception vector table (`vectors.S` + `exception.rs`); any unexpected trap routes through `exception_entry` and panics with a decoded ESR_EL1/ELR_EL1/FAR_EL1 dump
- `tools/qemu-run.sh` is the cargo runner: stages an ESP under `target/esp/`, auto-detects edk2 firmware, boots with `qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt` via `-drive file=fat:rw:...`
- **Milestone:** `cargo run -p minixrs-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release` boots through UEFI + Limine, prints `minix.rs booting on aarch64` + HHDM offset, then halts in `wfe`. Exception path verified by injecting a deliberate fault and observing a clean panic dump.

### Phase 2: Kernel IPC + Scheduling (the heart of MINIX)

Phase 2 is split into 6 PR-sized slices. Each slice is independently
buildable, boots, and produces observable output. The Phase 2 milestone
("two processes exchange IPC messages") is satisfied at the end of Slice
2.5; Slice 2.6 finishes the kernel-call surface needed by Phase 3.

- **Slice 2.1** ✓ shipped (PR #3, merged 2026-05-20) — `kernel-shared`
  foundation: `Message` (104-byte, 8-aligned `repr(C)`), `Endpoint`
  (generation-aware, 15-bit signed proc field, sentinels derived from
  `ENDPOINT_SLOT_TOP`), IPC primitive numbers, kernel-call numbers,
  task and server endpoint constants (renumbered contiguously; no
  static `LOG` slot), MINIX errno values. `NR_PROCS = 1024`. 21
  host-side unit tests; no kernel changes.
- **Slice 2.2** ✓ shipped (PR #4, merged 2026-05-22) — `Proc` and `Priv` structs with kernel-internal
  `RTS_*` / `MF_*` / priv-flag / trap-mask constants in
  `kernel/src/proc/flags.rs`; static `PROC_TABLE` (1029 slots) and
  `PRIV_TABLE` (64 slots) under `UnsafeCell` + `unsafe impl Sync` with
  documented single-threaded boot invariants; a 16-entry boot `IMAGE`
  drives `proc::init()`, and `proc::dump_tables()` writes a tabular
  UART view (5 kernel tasks runnable, 11 boot servers blocked on
  `RTS_NO_PRIV`, contiguous priv-ids 0–15). `kernel-shared` migrated
  `ProcNr` / `PrivId` / `SysId` from `i32` aliases to newtypes,
  added `NR_SYS_CALLS = 32` and a new `sys_limits` module
  (`NR_IO_RANGE`, `NR_IRQ`, `NR_MEM_RANGE`). `ArchRegisterFrame` stub
  in `arch/aarch64/context.rs` ready for slice 2.3 to populate. 25
  host-side tests pass (21 existing + 4 new newtype round-trips);
  clean release build with no warnings.
- **Slice 2.3** ✓ shipped (PR #5, merged 2026-05-22) — aarch64 SVC entry
  cooperative context switch. `entry.S`
  promotes the kernel from Limine's EL1t to EL1h on a primed SP_EL1
  (`mov x9, sp; msr SPSel, #1; mov sp, x9` — the only sequence that
  worked on QEMU virt / Cortex-A72; a bare `msr sp_el1, xN` from EL1t
  silently locks the core). New `arch/aarch64/{mmu,userland,trap,
  user_stub}.S/rs` build a minimal TTBR0_EL1 walk that maps one EL0
  code page (RO+X) and one EL0 stack page (RW+NX) at low VAs, copies a
  hand-coded `svc #0; b .` stub blob into the code page, and erets into
  EL0. Vector slot 8 (`el0_64_sync`) is specialized to `b
  el0_64_sync_entry` in `trap.S`; the SVC handler parks `&mut p.regs`
  in `TPIDR_EL1` so it can save state directly into the proc table and
  tail-call back to EL0 via `el1_return_to_user`. `do_ipc(frame)` in
  the new `kernel/src/ipc/` module prints each call and stores `OK` in
  `frame.x[0]`. `proc::sched::switch_to_user` wraps the eret. Verified
  in QEMU: `do_ipc[N]: call_nr=3 src_dst=32766 msg=0x0...` repeats
  ~16K times per second.
- **Slice 2.4** ✓ shipped (PR #6, merged 2026-05-23) — GICv3 + ARM generic timer + run queues. New
  `arch/aarch64/{gic,timer,irq}.rs` + `interrupt.S` bring up GICD/GICR
  (QEMU virt cortex-a72, `-machine virt,gic-version=3`), enable PPI 27
  (EL1 virtual timer) at 100 Hz, and route the IRQ through vector slot
  9 → `el0_64_irq_entry` (mirrors slice 2.3's slot-8 treatment) →
  `do_irq` → ICC_IAR1_EL1 ack / `clock::tick` / ICC_EOIR1_EL1. New
  `proc::sched` adds priority-banded FIFO run queues (`enqueue`,
  `dequeue`, `pick_proc`, `reschedule`, `run`) chained through
  `Proc::next_ready: Option<ProcNr>`. New `kernel/src/clock.rs` owns
  the per-tick handler — prints `current_proc.name[0]` to PL011, then
  decrements `quantum_left` and triggers `reschedule` on
  `RTS_NO_QUANTUM`. `userland_bootstrap` now stages two EL0 stubs (A
  and B at `0x40_0000`/`0x41_0000` sharing one code page, distinct
  stacks at `0x80_0000`/`0x81_0000`); SPSR drops to `0x340` so IRQs
  are unmasked at EL0. Verified in QEMU: 171 A ticks vs 170 B ticks
  over ~3.4 s, clean `AAAAA BBBBB AAAAA …` 5-per-quantum bursts with
  `do_ipc[N]` SVC traces interleaved (proves SVC + IRQ paths coexist).
- **Slice 2.5** ✓ shipped (PR #7, merged 2026-05-23) — IPC primitives in
  Rust. New `kernel/src/ipc/{mod,
  message, send, receive, notify, senda, deadlock}.rs`: `do_ipc`
  dispatcher with trap-mask gating, `mini_send` / `mini_receive` /
  `mini_notify` / `mini_sendnb` faithful to MINIX 3 `kernel/proc.c`,
  `deadlock_check` (size-2 SEND↔RECV legalization via the `function<<2`
  trick), `mini_senda` stub returning `ENOSYS` (deferred to a later
  slice; no Phase-2 consumer). New `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset`
  capture `nr` and end the borrow before calling `enqueue`/`dequeue`
  so RTS transitions stay run-queue-coherent. New `sched::schedule_next`
  picks the highest-priority runnable proc, parks its frame in
  `TPIDR_EL1`, and flushes any pending `MF_DELIVERMSG` into the user
  buffer at `Proc::deliver_msg_vir` — invoked from the SVC tail via the
  new `el1_svc_tail` shim (`trap.S` now reads `bl do_ipc; bl
  el1_svc_tail; b el1_return_to_user`) and from `reschedule` + `run`.
  `Proc` gains `deliver_msg_vir: u64`. User-memory IPC reads via
  `core::ptr::read_volatile` with a coarse `USER_VA_TOP = 1 << 48`
  bounds check; fault recovery is Phase 3. `user_stub.S` rewritten:
  two PC-relative-free blobs in separate sections — stub A
  (`SENDREC` to endpoint B, persistent counter in `x19`), stub B
  (`RECEIVE` from `ANY` then `SEND`-reply to `m_source`). `userland.rs`
  installs two physical code pages, four `map_4k` calls, plus
  `install_stub_privs` filling priv slots 16/17 with
  `trap_mask = SRV_T` and `ipc_to` cross-targeting A↔B. Verified in
  QEMU over 8 s: ~897K IPC ops; ~2990 each of `call=1`/`2`/`3` with
  every line `result=0`; no panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`; `A`/`B`
  clock-tick interleaving from slice 2.4 still visible. **Phase 2
  milestone reached.**
- **Slice 2.6** ✓ shipped (PR #8, merged 2026-05-25) — Kernel-call
  dispatch + minimum `SYS_*` set. New
  `kernel/src/system/{mod,do_getinfo,stubs}.rs` implement the MINIX 3
  fast-path shape: `ipc::dispatch`'s SENDREC arm detects
  `src_dst_e == boot_endpoint(SYSTEM)` and diverts to
  `system::kernel_call_sendrec`, which runs synchronously in the
  caller's EL1 context (mirrors `kernel/system.c::kernel_call`). The
  dispatcher applies `Priv::k_call_mask` gating and the same `ipc_to`
  permission check that `mini_send` does (re-exported `get_sys_bit`).
  `SYS_GETINFO` is real (`GET_WHOAMI` writes caller's endpoint,
  priv flags, and `name` into the payload in-place and returns `OK`);
  the other 13 Phase-2 `SYS_*` calls land as `ENOSYS` stubs with their
  canonical MINIX 3 `do_*` names so Phase 3+ can swap real handlers in
  without touching dispatch. A `const _: () = assert!(NR_KERN_CALLS_
  PHASE2 == 14)` next to the dispatch match locks arm coverage — adding
  a new `SYS_*` without a new arm is a compile error.
  `kernel-shared/callnr.rs` gains `GET_WHOAMI = 12` (matches MINIX 3
  `include/minix/sysinfo.h`) and `SYS_GETINFO_NAME_LEN = 16` (kernel's
  `PROC_NAME_LEN`; deviates from MINIX 3's 44 B because minix.rs never
  stores more than 16 B per slot). `user_stub.S` gains a third
  `.rodata.user_stub_c` blob — SENDREC to `ENDPOINT_SYS` (`0x7FFE` =
  `boot_endpoint(SYSTEM)`) with `m_type = SYS_GETINFO` and
  `payload[0..4] = GET_WHOAMI`, persistent counter in `x19`.
  `userland.rs` adds stub C's code/stack pages plus `STUB_C_PROC_NR`
  (13) / `STUB_C_PRIV_ID` (18). `install_stub_c_priv` differs from the
  slice-2.5 helper: `trap_mask = USR_T` (only SENDREC), `ipc_to`
  opened only to SYSTEM's priv slot (resolved at boot via
  `proc_table_ref()` so the IMAGE order isn't hard-coded), and
  `k_call_mask` opened only to `SYS_GETINFO`. `ipc/mod.rs`'s trace
  gains a `TRACE_HEAD = 12` head carve-out — C's fast-path rate
  (~125 K ops/sec) would otherwise drown A↔B's ~10 SVCs/sec in the
  modulo-100 sampling and the slice-2.5 ping-pong would look like it
  regressed; the head trace shows each stub's first SVC explicitly.
  Verified in QEMU over 8 s: SVC #1 = stub A SENDREC → B (result=0),
  SVCs #2–4 = stub B RECEIVE/SEND/RECEIVE (all result=0), SVCs #5+ =
  stub C SENDREC → SYSTEM with 6536 `[ksys N]` dispatches all
  `result=0`; clean — no `el0_sync_unexpected`, no panic.

Aggregate scope:

- `kernel-shared`: Message struct, Endpoint, all call numbers, error codes
- Process table (`Proc`), privilege table (`Priv`)
- IPC: `mini_send`, `mini_receive`, `mini_notify`, `deadlock_check`
- aarch64 arch: exception vectors, GICv3, ARM generic timer, SVC entry, context switch
- Run queues: `enqueue`, `dequeue`, `pick_proc`, `switch_to_user`
- Kernel calls: minimum set (SYS_GETINFO, SYS_PRIVCTL, SYS_FORK, SYS_EXEC, SYS_EXIT, SYS_COPY, SYS_SAFECOPY, SYS_IRQCTL, SYS_VMCTL, SYS_SCHEDULE, SYS_SETALARM, SYS_TIMES, SYS_DIAGCTL, SYS_SETGRANT)
- Boot image unpacking, load two test processes
- **Milestone:** Two processes exchange IPC messages ("ping-pong test")

### Phase 3: VM Server + Memory Management

Phase 3 is split into 7 PR-sized slices (decomposition tracked in
`~/.claude/plans/work-on-phase-3-optimized-petal.md`). Each slice
independently builds, boots, and prints observable progress. The Phase 3
milestone ("Boot processes each have isolated address spaces; VM handles
page faults") is satisfied at the end of slice 3.4; slices 3.5/3.6 then
add brk + mmap on top. POSIX fork and exec are deferred to Phase 4
(PM-driven).

Architecture choices (locked in by plan): per-process TTBR0 + 8-bit
ARMv8 ASIDs, kernel writes all user PTEs (VM passes decisions in via
SYS_VMCTL subcalls), kernel reads cross-AS user memory via HHDM after
walking the target proc's page table, VM uses static `[Region; N]`
per-proc tables (no allocator), stubs A/B/C from Phase 2.5/2.6 migrated
to per-proc TTBR0 in 3.1b and kept as regression coverage.

- **Slice 3.1a** ✓ shipped (PR #9, merged 2026-05-27) — Physical frame allocator + addrspace API, kernel-only,
  no EL0 changes. New `kernel/src/mm/{mod,frame}.rs`: intrusive free-list +
  per-region bump pointers seeded from Limine `MEMMAP_USABLE` entries
  (capacity `MAX_REGIONS = 16`; QEMU virt + Apple Silicon QEMU both fit
  comfortably). Frames inside the kernel image, embedded boot image, and
  Phase-2.5/2.6 static stub pages live in `EXECUTABLE_AND_MODULES` and
  are never visible to the allocator — no explicit reservation logic
  needed. `alloc_frame` zeros on hand-out so the caller never sees
  residual state; `free_frame` pushes via HHDM. `kernel-shared` /
  `Limine` integration: extended `arch/aarch64/limine.rs` with a
  `MemmapEntry` repr-C struct and a `memmap_entries()` iterator that
  walks the `**entry` indirection Limine uses. New
  `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/addrspace.rs`: `AddrSpace::new` allocates
  one L0 frame; `map_page(va, pa, Prot)` walks/allocates L1/L2/L3 on
  demand via the frame allocator, writes the leaf PTE through HHDM
  using the same PTE bit constants as `mmu.rs`; `walk_pt(va)` returns
  `Option<u64>`; `destroy()` recursively frees intermediate tables and
  the L0 root (leaf frames are caller-owned, not freed here). One-shot
  `mm_smoke_test` in `kmain` builds a throwaway AddrSpace, installs four
  mappings across distinct L2 slots, walks them all (plus one negative
  check), tears down, then verifies the free-list is LIFO by asserting
  the next `alloc_frame` returns the just-freed L0 PA. The smoke test is
  removed in 3.1b once real per-proc AddrSpaces replace `userland.rs`'s
  static `L0/L1/L2/L3_*` tables. Verified in QEMU over 8 s:
  `[mm] frame_alloc OK ttbr0_pa=0x40000000 / map OK / walk OK / free OK`
  prints in order; A↔B ping-pong head trace (`[ipc 1..4]`) and stub C
  SYS_GETINFO carve-out (~726 K SVCs, every line `result=0`) both
  unchanged from slice 2.6; no panic, no `el0_sync_unexpected`.
- **Slice 3.1b** ✓ shipped (PR #10, merged 2026-05-27) — Per-process
  TTBR0s + 8-bit ASIDs + minimal
  page-fault-diagnostic handler. `Proc` gains `ttbr0_pa: u64` and
  `asid: u8` (placed in a new "MMU state" block between `deliver_msg_vir`
  and `next_ready`; `Proc::EMPTY` zeroes both — kernel tasks and
  RTS_NO_PRIV boot servers keep the sentinel). New
  `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/asid.rs` carries an `UnsafeCell<u8>` counter
  starting at `FIRST_ASID = 1` (0 reserved for "uninitialized"), with
  `alloc_asid()` panicking on 8-bit wrap — real rollover deferred to
  Phase 4 since slice 3.1b only hands out three. `mmu.rs` loses the
  slice-2.3 monolithic `activate_user_ttbr0` (plus the slice-2.5 static
  `PageTable` newtype, `map_4k`, `pte_index`, `make_*_desc` const fns —
  all unused since 3.1a's `AddrSpace` took over) and gains three new
  helpers: `assert_tcr_el1_ttbr0_ready` now also asserts `TCR_EL1.AS == 0`;
  `enable_ttbr0_walks_once()` clears `TCR_EL1.EPD0` once at boot
  without binding any TTBR0; `switch_ttbr0_with_asid(ttbr0_pa, asid)`
  writes `TTBR0_EL1 = ttbr0_pa | ((asid as u64) << 48)` then issues
  `isb / tlbi aside1, Xt / dsb ish / isb` — TLBI is ASID-tagged and
  unconditional (the simpler control flow beats micro-optimizing three
  ASIDs). `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs` is rewritten end-to-end:
  every static `L0_TABLE` / `L1_TABLE` / `L2_TABLE` / `L3_CODE_TABLE` /

795	### Phase 4: Core Servers (PM, VFS, RS, DS, SCHED) + init
796	
797	Phase 4 is split into 8 PR-sized slices (decomposition tracked in
798	`~/.claude/plans/go-ahead-with-phase-dapper-rain.md`). Each slice independently

802	
803	Two scope decisions shape the phase: **exec is real but boot-embedded** (no
804	filesystem/musl until Phase 5, so `SYS_EXEC` loads ELF binaries packed into the
805	boot-image archive — the same archive that loads the servers — and Phase 5 later
806	swaps the source to a VFS file with no PM/kernel rework); and **scheduling moves
807	to a real user-space SCHED** by making the kernel scheduler *delegatable* rather
808	than replacing it (a per-proc `scheduler` endpoint defaults to kernel-scheduled,
809	and on quantum exhaustion the kernel either requeues or notifies the proc's
810	user-space scheduler). The boot `IMAGE` in `kernel/src/proc/table.rs` already

896	  through SEF (`[ipc 10/11] caller=9` GET_WHOAMI + DS publish); head traces show
897	  the round-trip — `[noq N] proc=C nr=13 -> scheduler=0x9` (kernel delegates) and
898	  `[ksys SYS_SCHEDULE] target=C nr=13 prio=8 quantum=5 result=0` (SCHED
899	  re-admits); stub C sustains (`[ksys]` `caller=13` to sample 250 800), A↔B
900	  ping-pong + D's three `[pf]` (brk×2 + mmap) resolved by VM all intact; every
901	  line `result=0`; zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`. Host: `cargo test
902	  -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-sched -p minixrs-server-rt` green; `cargo
903	  check --workspace` + clippy `-D warnings` + fmt clean.
904	- **Slice 4.4** ◀ next — RS (reincarnation server) + real `SYS_SETALARM`. Implement the
905	  per-proc one-shot alarm off the clock tick (currently ENOSYS). RS (already
906	  `ROOT_SYS_PROC` + `sig_mgr`) records the boot servers, arms a periodic alarm,
907	  and heartbeats/pings them on each tick; restart-on-crash is minimal (detect +
908	  log) in Phase 4.
909	- **Slice 4.5** — PM part A: process table + getpid + `SYS_PRIVCTL` + minimal
910	  signals. Stand up PM with a static `mproc` table, `getpid`/`getppid`, and a
911	  kill path whose default action is terminate (no handlers/sigaction/masks —
912	  that's beyond Phase 4). Make `SYS_PRIVCTL` real (set up a target's priv slot;
913	  the 4.6 fork path leans on it). This gives the Phase-3 VM "SIGSEGV leaves
914	  faulter blocked" path a real consequence (PM terminates the faulter).
915	- **Slice 4.6** — PM part B: fork + exit + wait. Kernel `SYS_FORK` (free slot,
916	  copy register frame, bump generation, alloc ASID, eager AS copy by walking the
917	  parent's TTBR0 + copying each user page via HHDM; CoW deferred) and `SYS_EXIT`
918	  (tear down AS via `AddrSpace::destroy`, free slot). Forked user procs share one
919	  USER priv slot (MINIX's `static_priv` model) so fork can't exhaust the 64-slot
920	  `PRIV_TABLE`. `VM_FORK` clones the parent's `ClientRegions` into the child. PM
921	  owns the tree: fork → `SYS_FORK` + `VM_FORK` + `SCHEDULING_START`; exit →
922	  zombie + SIGCHLD; `wait`/`waitpid` → reap.
923	- **Slice 4.7** — exec: `SYS_EXEC` + PM exec of a boot-embedded binary. Kernel
924	  builds a fresh `AddrSpace`, `elf::load_into` the archive module resolved by
925	  `module_by_name`, sets a minimal initial user stack, swaps `ttbr0_pa` +
926	  destroys the old AS, sets entry/SP. PM's `execve(name)` selects the embedded
927	  binary. A tiny freestanding "worker" ELF packed into the archive is the exec
928	  target.
929	- **Slice 4.8** — init (PID 1) + Phase 4 wrap-up + docs. **Phase 4 complete.**
930	  init (a freestanding Rust ELF loaded into `INIT_PROC_NR=10` by the archive)
931	  forks children that exec the worker, `wait`s, and respawns in a loop. Retire or
932	  demote the A/B/C/D stubs now that init + real procs are the live exercise. Real
933	  mdBook *Servers* chapter (`book/src/servers/overview.md`); CLAUDE.md Phase-4
934	  conventions; flip every slice marker and write the Phase-4-complete aggregate
935	  paragraph.
936	
937	Aggregate scope (Phase 4 as a whole):
938	
939	- `minix-ipc`: NOTIFY + SENDNB primitives
940	- `server-rt`: SEF startup + receive loop + init/signal callbacks (minimal subset)
941	- Multi-module boot-image archive (MXBI) + generalized server loader
942	- Kernel calls made real: `SYS_FORK`, `SYS_EXEC`, `SYS_EXIT`, `SYS_PRIVCTL`,
943	  `SYS_SCHEDULE`, `SYS_SETALARM`, plus new `SYS_SCHEDCTL` and a minimal signal
944	  mechanism; delegatable scheduler (`Proc::scheduler`)
945	- Servers (all static-allocation, no heap): DS (endpoint registry), SCHED (real
946	  user-space policy), RS (heartbeat monitor), VFS (skeletal boot), PM (process
947	  table, fork, exec, exit, wait, getpid, minimal signals)
948	- init (PID 1) forking/exec/waiting; embedded "worker" binary as the exec target
949	- **Milestone:** Full server boot sequence completes; init process starts
950	
951	### Phase 5: musl Fork + File Systems
952	
953	- Add `src/minix/` to musl fork with IPC wrappers
954	- Generate C headers from `kernel-shared` via cbindgen
955	- `fs/mfs/`: MINIX File System server (Rust, MinixFS v3 on-disk format)
956	- `fs/pfs/`: Pipe File System
957	- `drivers/memory/`: /dev/null, /dev/zero, ramdisk
958	- initramfs for early boot before disk driver
959	- **Milestone:** C "Hello World" compiled against musl runs on minix.rs
960	
961	### Phase 6: VirtIO Drivers
962	
963	- `drivers/driver-rt/`: VirtIO MMIO transport (aarch64), virtqueue management, BDEV/CDEV protocol
964	- `drivers/virtio-blk/`: Block device
965	- `drivers/virtio-console/`: TTY
966	- `drivers/virtio-net/`: Network (packet I/O only; TCP/IP stack is later)
967	- Root filesystem on VirtIO disk
968	- **Milestone:** Boots from VirtIO disk, mounts MinixFS root
969	
970	### Phase 7: Userland
971	
972	- `userland/init/`: Run /etc/rc, respawn gettys
973	- `userland/sh/`: Simple shell (pipes, redirects, background jobs, builtins)
974	- `userland/coreutils/`: Multi-call binary -- ls, cat, cp, mv, rm, mkdir, rmdir, echo, wc, head, tail, grep, chmod, pwd, env, sleep, date, uname, kill, ps, true, false, test
975	- **Milestone:** Login, navigate filesystem, run scripts
976	
977	### Phase 8: x86_64 Port
978	
979	- `kernel/src/arch/x86_64/`: GDT, IDT, APIC, SYSCALL/SYSRET, 4-level page tables, context switch
980	- `musl/src/minix/_ipc_x86_64.S`
981	- VirtIO PCI transport
982	- `tools/qemu-run-x86_64.sh`
983	- **Milestone:** Same minix.rs boots on `qemu-system-x86_64`
984	
985	### Phase 9: Documentation + Polish
986	
987	- Comprehensive rustdoc across all crates
988	- Architecture guide for students
989	- End-to-end syscall trace walkthrough ("How read() works")
990	- IPC latency + context switch benchmarks
991	- Test suite (kernel unit tests, IPC stress, POSIX conformance)
992	- **Milestone:** Teaching-ready OS with documentation
993	
994	---
995	
996	## Key Design Decisions
997	
998	**Rust kernel, not incremental port:** MINIX 3's C kernel uses raw pointers, global mutable state, and macro-heavy abstractions that resist incremental Rustification. A greenfield kernel uses proper Rust patterns from the start (enums for flags, Result for errors, indices not pointers for linked lists).
999	
1000	**`unsafe` boundaries in kernel:** (a) Process table access (`UnsafeCell` static array), (b) user-space memory copies, (c) hardware register access (inline asm), (d) assembly entry/exit paths. Each `unsafe` block gets a `// SAFETY:` comment.
1001	
1002	**musl over Rust libc:** musl is well-tested, BSD-compatible, and complete. The MINIX-specific work is confined to ~100 syscall wrappers + IPC assembly stub. The rest of musl (stdio, string, math, locale) works unchanged.
1003	
1004	**Servers in Rust:** Shared `kernel-shared` message types provide compile-time IPC protocol verification. C servers (linked against musl) also work since the IPC mechanism is language-agnostic.
1005	
1006	**Multi-call coreutils:** Single `coreutils` binary dispatching on argv[0] (like BusyBox) minimizes disk space and simplifies building.
1007	
1008	---
1009	
1010	## Verification Strategy
1011	
1012	Each phase has a concrete milestone testable in QEMU (aarch64 primary):
1013	
1014	- Phase 0: `docs/` complete, directory tree navigable, CLAUDE.md in place
1015	- Phase 1: `qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt` -> serial output visible
1016	- Phase 2: Ping-pong test via IPC verified by serial trace
1017	- Phase 3: Boot processes run in separate address spaces
1018	- Phase 4: Server initialization sequence completes to init
1019	- Phase 5: `printf("Hello\n")` from C program reaches serial
1020	- Phase 6: Boots from VirtIO disk, mounts MinixFS root
1021	- Phase 7: Interactive shell session
1022	- Phase 8: Same tests pass on `qemu-system-x86_64`
1023	- Phase 9: `cargo doc`, test suite green
1024	
1025	QEMU flags for aarch64 dev:
1026	```
1027	qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt -cpu cortex-a72 -m 256M \
1028	  -serial stdio -no-reboot -d int,cpu_reset -D qemu.log \
1029	  -drive file=minixrs.img,format=raw,if=virtio \
1030	  -device virtio-net-device
1031	```
1032	GDB: add `-s -S` then `rust-gdb -ex "target remote :1234"`
1033	
1034	---
1035	
1036	## Critical Reference Files (MINIX 3)
1037	
1038	Paths are relative to the MINIX 3 source tree root (under the `minix/` subdirectory
1039	for MINIX-specific code). See https://github.com/Stichting-MINIX-Research-Foundation/minix
1040	
1041	| Purpose | Path in MINIX 3 tree |
1042	|---------|---------------------|
1043	| IPC implementation (translate to Rust) | `kernel/proc.c` |
1044	| Process/privilege structures | `kernel/proc.h`, `kernel/priv.h` |
1045	| Kernel call dispatch | `kernel/system.c` |
1046	| x86_64 entry points | `kernel/arch/x86_64/mpx.S` |
1047	| Message definitions | `include/minix/ipc.h` |
1048	| IPC constants | `include/minix/ipcconst.h` |
1049	| Server endpoints + call numbers | `include/minix/com.h`, `include/minix/callnr.h` |
1050	| User-space IPC stubs | `lib/libc/arch/x86_64/sys/_ipc.S` |
1051	| _syscall() wrapper | `lib/libc/sys/syscall.c` |
1052	| POSIX wrappers (template for musl) | `lib/libc/sys/*.c` |
1053	| SEF framework | `lib/libsys/sef.c` |
1054	| Boot process table | `kernel/table.c` |
1055	| Limine integration design | `BOOT.md` (at repo root) |

brk, mmap (all static-allocation; no heap allocator in VM)
- **Milestone:** Boot processes each have isolated address spaces; VM
  handles page faults

### Phase 4: Core Servers (PM, VFS, RS, DS, SCHED) + init

Phase 4 is split into 8 PR-sized slices (decomposition tracked in
`~/.claude/plans/go-ahead-with-phase-dapper-rain.md`). Each slice independently
builds, boots, and prints observable progress — same cadence as Phases 2–3. The
Phase 4 milestone ("Full server boot sequence completes; init process starts")
is satisfied at the end of slice 4.8.

Two scope decisions shape the phase: **exec is real but boot-embedded** (no
filesystem/musl until Phase 5, so `SYS_EXEC` loads ELF binaries packed into the
boot-image archive — the same archive that loads the servers — and Phase 5 later
swaps the source to a VFS file with no PM/kernel rework); and **scheduling moves
to a real user-space SCHED** by making the kernel scheduler *delegatable* rather
than replacing it (a per-proc `scheduler` endpoint defaults to kernel-scheduled,
and on quantum exhaustion the kernel either requeues or notifies the proc's
user-space scheduler). The boot `IMAGE` in `kernel/src/proc/table.rs` already
lists pm/vfs/rs/ds/sched/init with correct priv flags, and `init_boot_image`
already fills their `ipc_to` / `k_call_mask`, so loading a server needs only an
ELF + the generalized `load_boot_server` path — no new boot priv wiring.

- **Slice 4.1** ✓ shipped (PR #23, merged 2026-06-14) —
  `server-rt` SEF framework + migrate VM onto it + finish
  `minix-ipc`. Add `ipc_notify` / `ipc_sendnb` (new SVC `primitive` values).
  Build `server-rt`: `sef_startup()` (learn own endpoint/name via
  `SYS_GETINFO(GET_WHOAMI)`, run `init_fresh` callback) and `sef_receive()`
  (wrap `ipc_receive(ANY, …)`, intercept SEF control messages — ping/signal/init
  — and return only application messages), with static function-pointer callback
  registration (no heap; minimal subset vs MINIX `lib/libsys/sef.c`). Port
  `servers/vm` to the SEF loop (handlers unchanged) so VM is live regression
  coverage for the framework before any new server depends on it.
- **Slice 4.2** ✓ shipped (PR #24, merged 2026-06-21) — Multi-module boot image
  + DS server + VFS skeletal boot.
  `kernel/build.rs`'s single-VM embed is generalized into `build_server(name,
  dir, …)` + `pack_mxbi(...)`: it builds each server (VM/DS/VFS) into its own
  isolated `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, packs the ELFs into one MXBI archive in `OUT_DIR`
  (16-byte header `magic "MXBI"/ver/count/total_size` + 32-byte records
  `{proc_nr:i32, offset:u32, len:u32, name:[u8;20]}` + back-to-back payloads, all
  LE), and emits `BOOT_IMAGE_PATH` (replacing `VM_ELF_PATH`). `boot_image/mod.rs`
  becomes a zero-copy `BootImage` view (`include_bytes!` of the archive) exposing
  `iter()` → `(ProcNr, &[u8])` for the load loop plus `module_by_proc_nr` /
  `module_by_name` (the latter `#[allow(dead_code)]` until exec in 4.7); the whole
  module stays `cfg(target_os="none")` so host `cargo check`/`test` (where
  `BOOT_IMAGE_PATH` is unset) never evaluate the `env!`. `userland.rs::vm_bootstrap`
  is refactored into `load_boot_server(nr, elf, stack_va)` looped over
  `BootImage::iter()`; all servers share one `SERVER_STACK_VA` (each has its own
  TTBR0). No new boot priv wiring — `init_boot_image` already grants DS(5)/VFS(1)/
  VM(7) `SRV_T` `ipc_to` over `[0, n_active)`. New `kernel-shared/callnr.rs`
  `DS_RQ_BASE = 0xE00` + `DS_PUBLISH`/`DS_RETRIEVE`/`DS_CHECK` + `NR_DS_REQUESTS`
  (const-asserted distinct from VM/SEF, below `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`) + host tests. DS
  (`servers/ds`): a static `[Entry; 16]` name→endpoint registry
  (`servers/ds/src/registry.rs`, `UnsafeCell` newtype like `vm/region.rs`, pure
  `publish/retrieve/check` host-tested) driven by a SEF loop; key = 16-byte
  NUL-padded server name in payload `0..16`, endpoint i32 in `16..20`. DS seeds
  its *own* entry in-process in `ds_init` (a self-SENDREC would deadlock). VFS
  (`servers/vfs`): skeletal SEF boot that drops application traffic. A shared
  `server-rt::sef_publish_to_ds(endpoint, name)` helper (`init_fresh` body for
  VM + VFS, coverage-excluded with `sef.rs`) marshals `DS_PUBLISH`. Verified in
  QEMU over 10 s: seven `[as]` lines (vm/ds/vfs asid 1–3, stubs A–D asid 4–7);
  head `[ipc]` shows VM→SYSTEM + VM→DS publish (DS replies VM), DS→SYSTEM +
  RECEIVE(ANY) (no DS self-publish), VFS→SYSTEM + VFS→DS publish, then A↔B
  ping-pong; stub C `SYS_GETINFO` (`[ksys] call=0`) and stub D's three `[pf]`
  (brk×2 + mmap) resolved by VM (`VMCTL_PT_MAP`×3 + one `VMCTL_PT_UNMAP`) all
  intact; 3319 `[ipc]` + 3310 `[ksys]` + 3 `[pf]`, every line `result=0`; zero
  panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`. Host: `cargo test -p minixrs-kernel-shared`
  (DS callnr) + `-p minixrs-ds` (registry) green; `cargo check --workspace` +
  clippy `-D warnings` + fmt clean.
- **Slice 4.3** ◀ ready (branch `feature/phase-4-3-sched`, pending merge) — Real
  user-space scheduling: kernel delegatable scheduler + SCHED server (single PR).
  `Proc` gains `scheduler: Endpoint` (`NONE` = kernel-scheduled, the boot
  default; `populate_proc` sets it and `Proc::EMPTY` zeroes it to `NONE`).
  `sched::reschedule` branches on it: `NONE` keeps the slice-2.4 refill+rotate;
  otherwise it dequeues the preempted proc (which `clock::tick`'s bare `fetch_or`
  left enqueued), leaves `RTS_NO_QUANTUM` set, and sends `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM`
  to the scheduler via new `ipc::send::mini_sched_no_quantum_send` (a near-clone
  of 3.4's `mini_pf_send`, wrapped by `ipc::send_no_quantum` which materializes
  the proc-table slice like `send_pagefault_to_vm`). `SYS_SCHEDULE` becomes real
  and a new `SYS_SCHEDCTL` lands (`kernel/src/system/do_schedule.rs`), both
  target-taking and routed beside `SYS_VMCTL` in `kernel_call_dispatch`:
  `do_schedule` sets a target's priority/quantum and re-admits it
  (`rts_unset(RTS_NO_QUANTUM)` if off-queue, else dequeue+enqueue for the band
  move); `do_schedctl` claims (`scheduler = caller`) or releases
  (`SCHEDCTL_FLAG_KERNEL` → `NONE`) a target. `kernel-shared/callnr.rs` gains
  `SYS_SCHEDCTL`, `SCHEDCTL_FLAG_KERNEL`, `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 = 15` (one-slice
  `_PHASE3` alias, dropped in 4.4), and a `SCHED_RQ_BASE = 0xF00` range
  (`SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM`/`START`/`STOP`/`SET_NICE`, `NR_SCHED_MSGS = 4`,
  const-asserted distinct from VM/SEF/DS and below `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`) + host
  tests; `init_boot_image`'s `k_call_mask` fill widens to `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4`
  so SCHED may issue the two calls. SCHED (`servers/sched`, server-rt based):
  SEF loop publishing to DS, handlers for all four `SCHEDULING_*`; the policy is
  a round-robin quantum refresh at a fixed managed band (`USER_Q = 8`, the
  boot-server band, so a CPU-bound managed proc round-robins instead of sinking
  behind the kernel-scheduled stubs) held in a static `[SchedProc; 16]`
  `UnsafeCell` table (`servers/sched/src/policy.rs`, pure helpers host-tested
  like `ds/registry.rs`). MINIX-style priority aging (drop a band + periodic
  `balance_queues` boost) is deferred to 4.4's `SYS_SETALARM` — without the boost,
  dropping a band would starve the managed proc behind the band-8 stubs.
  `SCHEDULING_START`/`STOP`/`SET_NICE` are implemented for PM/RS to drive from
  4.5+; in 4.3 the kernel pre-delegates stub C (`userland.rs` sets its
  `scheduler = boot_endpoint(SCHED_PROC_NR)`), the live exercise. Added to the
  MXBI `servers` array in `build.rs` (proc_nr 9). Verified in QEMU over 10 s:
  eight `[as]` lines (vm/ds/vfs/sched asid 1–4, stubs A–D asid 5–8); SCHED boots
  through SEF (`[ipc 10/11] caller=9` GET_WHOAMI + DS publish); head traces show
  the round-trip — `[noq N] proc=C nr=13 -> scheduler=0x9` (kernel delegates) and
  `[ksys SYS_SCHEDULE] target=C nr=13 prio=8 quantum=5 result=0` (SCHED
  re-admits); stub C sustains (`[ksys]` `caller=13` to sample 250 800), A↔B
  ping-pong + D's three `[pf]` (brk×2 + mmap) resolved by VM all intact; every
  line `result=0`; zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`. Host: `cargo test
  -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-sched -p minixrs-server-rt` green; `cargo
  check --workspace` + clippy `-D warnings` + fmt clean.
- **Slice 4.4** ◀ next — RS (reincarnation server) + real `SYS_SETALARM`. Implement the
  per-proc one-shot alarm off the clock tick (currently ENOSYS). RS (already
  `ROOT_SYS_PROC` + `sig_mgr`) records the boot servers, arms a periodic alarm,
  and heartbeats/pings them on each tick; restart-on-crash is minimal (detect +
  log) in Phase 4.
- **Slice 4.5** — PM part A: process table + getpid + `SYS_PRIVCTL` + minimal
  signals. Stand up PM with a static `mproc` table, `getpid`/`getppid`, and a
  kill path whose default action is terminate (no handlers/sigaction/masks —
  that's beyond Phase 4). Make `SYS_PRIVCTL` real (set up a target's priv slot;
  the 4.6 fork path leans on it). This gives the Phase-3 VM "SIGSEGV leaves
  faulter blocked" path a real consequence (PM terminates the faulter).
- **Slice 4.6** — PM part B: fork + exit + wait. Kernel `SYS_FORK` (free slot,
  copy register frame, bump generation, alloc ASID, eager AS copy by walking the
  parent's TTBR0 + copying each user page via HHDM; CoW deferred) and `SYS_EXIT`
  (tear down AS via `AddrSpace::destroy`, free slot). Forked user procs share one
  USER priv slot (MINIX's `static_priv` model) so fork can't exhaust the 64-slot
  `PRIV_TABLE`. `VM_FORK` clones the parent's `ClientRegions` into the child. PM
  owns the tree: fork → `SYS_FORK` + `VM_FORK` + `SCHEDULING_START`; exit →
  zombie + SIGCHLD; `wait`/`waitpid` → reap.
- **Slice 4.7** — exec: `SYS_EXEC` + PM exec of a boot-embedded binary. Kernel
  builds a fresh `AddrSpace`, `elf::load_into` the archive module resolved by
  `module_by_name`, sets a minimal initial user stack, swaps `ttbr0_pa` +
  destroys the old AS, sets entry/SP. PM's `execve(name)` selects the embedded
  binary. A tiny freestanding "worker" ELF packed into the archive is the exec
  target.
- **Slice 4.8** — init (PID 1) + Phase 4 wrap-up + docs. **Phase 4 complete.**
  init (a freestanding Rust ELF loaded into `INIT_PROC_NR=10` by the archive)
  forks children that exec the worker, `wait`s, and respawns in a loop. Retire or
  demote the A/B/C/D stubs now that init + real procs are the live exercise. Real
  mdBook *Servers* chapter (`book/src/servers/overview.md`); CLAUDE.md Phase-4
  conventions; flip every slice marker and write the Phase-4-complete aggregate
  paragraph.

Aggregate scope (Phase 4 as a whole):

- `minix-ipc`: NOTIFY + SENDNB primitives
- `server-rt`: SEF startup + receive loop + init/signal callbacks (minimal subset)
- Multi-module boot-image archive (MXBI) + generalized server loader
- Kernel calls made real: `SYS_FORK`, `SYS_EXEC`, `SYS_EXIT`, `SYS_PRIVCTL`,
  `SYS_SCHEDULE`, `SYS_SETALARM`, plus new `SYS_SCHEDCTL` and a minimal signal
  mechanism; delegatable scheduler (`Proc::scheduler`)
- Servers (all static-allocation, no heap): DS (endpoint registry), SCHED (real
  user-space policy), RS (heartbeat monitor), VFS (skeletal boot), PM (process
  table, fork, exec, exit, wait, getpid, minimal signals)
- init (PID 1) forking/exec/waiting; embedded "worker" binary as the exec target
- **Milestone:** Full server boot sequence completes; init process starts

---

## Key Design Decisions

**Rust kernel, not incremental port:** MINIX 3's C kernel uses raw pointers, global mutable state, and macro-heavy abstractions that resist incremental Rustification. A greenfield kernel uses proper Rust patterns from the start (enums for flags, Result for errors, indices not pointers for linked lists).

**`unsafe` boundaries in kernel:** (a) Process table access (`UnsafeCell` static array), (b) user-space memory copies, (c) hardware register access (inline asm), (d) assembly entry/exit paths. Each `unsafe` block gets a `// SAFETY:` comment.

**musl over Rust libc:** musl is well-tested, BSD-compatible, and complete. The MINIX-specific work is confined to ~100 syscall wrappers + IPC assembly stub. The rest of musl (stdio, string, math, locale) works unchanged.

**Servers in Rust:** Shared `kernel-shared` message types provide compile-time IPC protocol verification. C servers (linked against musl) also work since the IPC mechanism is language-agnostic.

**Multi-call coreutils:** Single `coreutils` binary dispatching on argv[0] (like BusyBox) minimizes disk space and simplifies building.

---

## Verification Strategy

Each phase has a concrete milestone testable in QEMU (aarch64 primary):

- Phase 0: `docs/` complete, directory tree navigable, CLAUDE.md in place
- Phase 1: `qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt` -> serial output visible
- Phase 2: Ping-pong test via IPC verified by serial trace
- Phase 3: Boot processes run in separate address spaces
- Phase 4: Server initialization sequence completes to init
- Phase 5: `printf("Hello\n")` from C program reaches serial
- Phase 6: Boots from VirtIO disk, mounts MinixFS root
- Phase 7: Interactive shell session
- Phase 8: Same tests pass on `qemu-system-x86_64`
- Phase 9: `cargo doc`, test suite green

QEMU flags for aarch64 dev:
```
qemu-system-aarch64 -M virt -cpu cortex-a72 -m 256M \
  -serial stdio -no-reboot -d int,cpu_reset -D qemu.log \
  -drive file=minixrs.img,format=raw,if=virtio \
  -device virtio-net-device
```
GDB: add `-s -S` then `rust-gdb -ex "target remote :1234"`

---

## Critical Reference Files (MINIX 3)

Paths are relative to the MINIX 3 source tree root (under the `minix/` subdirectory
for MINIX-specific code). See https://github.com/Stichting-MINIX-Research-Foundation/minix

| Purpose | Path in MINIX 3 tree |
|---------|---------------------|
| IPC implementation (translate to Rust) | `kernel/proc.c` |
| Process/privilege structures | `kernel/proc.h`, `kernel/priv.h` |
| Kernel call dispatch | `kernel/system.c` |
| x86_64 entry points | `kernel/arch/x86_64/mpx.S` |
| Message definitions | `include/minix/ipc.h` |
| IPC constants | `include/minix/ipcconst.h` |
| Server endpoints + call numbers | `include/minix/com.h`, `include/minix/callnr.h` |
| User-space IPC stubs | `lib/libc/arch/x86_64/sys/_ipc.S` |
| _syscall() wrapper | `lib/libc/sys/syscall.c` |
| POSIX wrappers (template for musl) | `lib/libc/sys/*.c` |
| SEF framework | `lib/libsys/sef.c` |
| Boot process table | `kernel/table.c` |
| Limine integration design | `BOOT.md` (at repo root) |

1	# Phase 4: Core Servers (PM, VFS, RS, DS, SCHED) + init
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Phases 1–3 (all shipped) gave minix.rs a working microkernel with a single
6	real user-space server. Today the kernel does IPC, GICv3+timer scheduling
7	(priority-banded FIFO, fully kernel-driven), SVC kernel-call dispatch with real
8	`SYS_GETINFO` + `SYS_VMCTL`, per-process TTBR0/ASID address spaces, an EL0
9	page-fault handler that routes to **VM** (`servers/vm`, the one working server),
10	and brk/mmap/munmap. Four hand-coded EL0 stubs (A/B/C/D) exercise IPC,
11	SYS_GETINFO, and the fault→VM path as regression coverage.
12	
13	Everything else named in the boot image is inert: `kernel/src/proc/table.rs`'s
14	`IMAGE` already lists **pm, vfs, rs, memory, tty, ds, mfs, vm, pfs, sched, init**
15	with correct priorities/quantum/priv-flags, and `init_boot_image` already wires
16	each `SRV_T` server's `ipc_to` (all 16 boot slots) and `k_call_mask` (all 14
17	kernel calls). But pm/vfs/rs/ds/sched/init have **no ELF, no TTBR0**, and sit
18	blocked on `RTS_NO_PRIV`. `server-rt` is a 5-line placeholder. `minix-ipc`
19	exposes only SEND/RECEIVE/SENDREC. The boot loader is hardcoded to one module
20	(`include_bytes!(env!("VM_ELF_PATH"))`); the multi-module MXBI archive was
21	explicitly deferred "until Phase 4 loads more than one server." Of the 14 kernel
22	calls, 12 are still `ENOSYS` stubs (fork/exec/exit/privctl/schedule/setalarm/
23	times/copy/safecopy/irqctl/diagctl/setgrant).
24	
25	Phase 4 delivers the milestone **"Full server boot sequence completes; init
26	process starts."** That means: the SEF framework (`server-rt`) exists and every
27	core server boots through it; DS/RS/SCHED/VFS/PM all come up via a multi-module
28	boot image and register/heartbeat; PM implements the process lifecycle (fork,
29	exec, exit, wait, getpid, minimal signals); and **init runs as PID 1**, forking
30	children that exec and exit, with PM reaping them in a loop.
31	
32	**Scope decisions locked in with the user (2026-06-14):**
33	- **exec = real, boot-embedded.** No filesystem/musl until Phase 5, so `SYS_EXEC`
34	  loads ELF binaries packed into the boot-image archive (the same archive that
35	  loads the servers doubles as the executable store), like RS launching a stored
36	  service. Phase 5 swaps the source to a VFS file with no PM/kernel rework.
37	- **Scheduling = real user-space SCHED.** The kernel scheduler is made
38	  *delegatable*, not replaced: a per-proc `scheduler` endpoint defaults to
39	  kernel-scheduled (`NONE`); on quantum exhaustion the kernel either requeues
40	  (kernel-scheduled) or notifies the proc's user-space scheduler and leaves it
41	  not-runnable until `SYS_SCHEDULE` re-admits it. `SYS_SCHEDULE` becomes real and
42	  a new `SYS_SCHEDCTL` lets SCHED claim/release procs. Faithful to MINIX's
43	  distinctive design while keeping the proven run-queue as the kernel-scheduled
44	  fallback.
45	- **This session delivers the decomposition only** — write the Phase 4 slice
46	  breakdown into `docs/plan.md` (+ keep this detailed plan file). Implement the
47	  slices in later sessions, one PR each, matching the Phase 2/3 cadence.
48	
49	**Architecture choices (carried from Phase 3, applied to Phase 4):**
50	- **Kernel owns frames + all user PTE writes.** fork's address-space copy and
51	  exec's address-space build are kernel operations (`SYS_FORK`/`SYS_EXEC` walk/
52	  alloc/copy via HHDM); servers drive *policy*, never touch page tables. VM keeps
53	  its region bookkeeping and clones it on fork via a `VM_FORK` request.
54	- **No heap allocator in servers.** PM's `mproc`, SCHED's per-proc state, DS's
55	  key/value store, RS's service table are all static arrays in `UnsafeCell`
56	  newtypes, mirroring VM's `region.rs` and the kernel's `PROC_TABLE` convention.
57	- **Forked user processes share one USER priv slot** (MINIX's `static_priv`
58	  model): unprivileged trap mask (SENDREC only), `ipc_to` opened to PM/VFS/VM,
59	  no kernel calls. Only system processes consume individual priv slots — fork
60	  must not exhaust the 64-slot `PRIV_TABLE`.
61	- **Stubs A/B/C/D stay** as regression coverage through Phase 4, retired (or
62	  demoted) only in the final slice once init + real procs are the live exercise.
63	
64	---
65	
66	## Slice plan (8 PR-sized slices)
67	
68	Each slice independently builds, boots in QEMU (`aarch64-unknown-none`,
69	`timeout 8 cargo run …`), and prints observable progress. Same cadence as Phases
70	2–3. Update `docs/plan.md` slice markers (`◀ next` / `◀ ready (branch …)` /
71	`✓ shipped (PR #N, …)`) as part of each PR.
72	
73	### Slice 4.1 — `server-rt` SEF framework + migrate VM + finish `minix-ipc`
74	
75	Foundation: the System Event Framework every server links against, proven by
76	porting the one working server to it.
77	- **`minix-ipc`**: add `ipc_notify` and `ipc_sendnb` (the SVC trap already
78	  exists — these are new `primitive` values: NOTIFY/SENDNB). `ipc_senda` stays
79	  deferred (no Phase-4 consumer).
80	- **`server-rt`** (`src/{lib,sef,init,signal}.rs`): `sef_startup()` learns the
81	  server's own endpoint + name via `SYS_GETINFO(GET_WHOAMI)` (already real),
82	  runs the registered `init_fresh` callback. `sef_receive(&mut Message)` wraps
83	  `ipc_receive(ANY, …)` and intercepts SEF control messages — heartbeat/ping
84	  (reply immediately), signal (dispatch to `signal_handler` callback), init —
85	  returning only application messages to the caller. Callback registration via a
86	  small static function-pointer table (no heap). Document the minimal subset vs
87	  MINIX `lib/libsys/sef.c` (live-update / state-transfer deferred).
88	- **Migrate `servers/vm`** to drive its loop through `sef_startup` + `sef_receive`
89	  instead of the hand-rolled `loop { ipc_receive(ANY, …) }`. VM's handlers
90	  (`handle_pagefault`/`brk`/`mmap`/`munmap`) are unchanged; only the loop
91	  scaffolding moves. This keeps VM as live regression coverage and validates the
92	  framework against a real consumer before any new server depends on it.
93	- **Files**: `minix-ipc/src/lib.rs`; new `server-rt/src/{sef,init,signal}.rs`,
94	  `server-rt/src/lib.rs`; `servers/vm/src/main.rs`; `servers/vm/Cargo.toml` (add
95	  `minixrs-server-rt`); `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs` (SEF control-message
96	  numbers — ping/signal/init); host tests in `server-rt`.
97	- **Verify**: VM still resolves stub D's faults and A/B/C/D run unchanged; boot
98	  trace shows VM passing through SEF startup (a whoami-derived line) then its
99	  receive loop. `cargo test` green for the new `server-rt` logic.
100	
101	### Slice 4.2 — Multi-module boot image + DS server + VFS skeletal boot
102	
103	The infrastructure that lets the kernel load N servers, plus the two cheapest
104	SEF servers to prove it.
105	- **Boot-image archive (MXBI)**: generalize `kernel/build.rs`'s
106	  `build_vm_server` into `build_server(name, crate, user.ld)` and build vm + ds +
107	  vfs (more added per later slice). Pack the resulting ELFs into a single
108	  `.boot_image` archive blob with a header table `{name, proc_nr, offset, len}`
109	  (the format `docs/boot.md` sketches). `kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs` reads the
110	  archive and exposes `module_by_proc_nr(nr)` / `module_by_name(name)` (the
111	  latter is reused by exec in 4.7). `boot_image/elf.rs` (`load_into`) is
112	  unchanged.
113	- **Generalized loader**: refactor `userland.rs::vm_bootstrap` into
114	  `load_boot_server(proc_nr, elf)` — `AddrSpace::new`, `elf::load_into`, alloc +
115	  map a stack frame, set `elr_el1`/`sp_el0`/`spsr`/`ttbr0_pa`/`asid`, clear
116	  `RTS_NO_PRIV`, `enqueue`. Priv wiring is already done by `init_boot_image`, so
117	  no per-server priv code is needed for boot servers. Loop the loader over the
118	  archive's server modules.
119	- **DS server** (`servers/ds`, server-rt based): static key→value/endpoint map
120	  (`UnsafeCell` newtype, fixed capacity). `DS_PUBLISH` / `DS_RETRIEVE` /
121	  `DS_CHECK`. Every server publishes its own endpoint at `init_fresh`.
122	- **VFS skeletal boot** (`servers/vfs`): boots through SEF, publishes its
123	  endpoint, static `fproc` table stub, replies to pings — **no file ops** (the
124	  PM↔VFS fork/exec/exit work protocol needs FDs and is Phase 5). It is in the
125	  boot sequence, so it must come up; its real work is deferred.
126	- **Files**: `kernel/build.rs`; `kernel/src/boot_image/mod.rs` (+ new
127	  `boot_image/archive.rs`); new `servers/ds/{Cargo.toml,user.ld,src/main.rs}`;
128	  new `servers/vfs/{…}`; `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs`;
129	  `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs` (DS_* numbers).
130	- **Verify**: boot trace shows vm + ds + vfs each through SEF startup; a test
131	  stub (or VM) publishes + retrieves an endpoint via DS and gets `result=0`;
132	  A/B/C/D + VM fault path unchanged.
133	
134	### Slice 4.3 — Real user-space scheduling (kernel delegatable scheduler + SCHED)
135	
136	Make the kernel scheduler delegatable and stand up the SCHED server. Mirror
137	3.4's a/b split if it keeps PRs reviewable.
138	- **4.3a (kernel)**: `Proc` gains `scheduler: Endpoint` (`NONE` = kernel-
139	  scheduled; boot procs default to it). In `clock::tick`/`sched::reschedule`, on
140	  `RTS_NO_QUANTUM`: if `scheduler == NONE` requeue as today; else send a
141	  `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM` message to `scheduler` (kernel-originated, like 3.4's
142	  `mini_pf_send`) and leave the proc not-runnable. `SYS_SCHEDULE` becomes real
143	  (set target priority + quantum, enqueue); new kernel call `SYS_SCHEDCTL` lets a
144	  scheduler claim/release a target (sets/clears its `scheduler`). Bump the kernel-
145	  call count to `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` (+1 for SCHEDCTL), update the dispatch
146	  const-assert, keep a one-slice `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE3` alias (drop next slice),
147	  widen `init_boot_image`'s `k_call_mask` fill to the new count.
148	- **4.3b (server)**: `servers/sched` (server-rt based): `SCHEDULING_START`
149	  (claim a proc via `SYS_SCHEDCTL`, assign initial priority/quantum via
150	  `SYS_SCHEDULE`), `SCHEDULING_STOP`, `SCHEDULING_SET_NICE`,
151	  `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM` (simple MINIX-style policy: drop a priority band on
152	  full-quantum use, then `SYS_SCHEDULE`). Flip the boot servers (or a test stub)
153	  to SCHED-scheduled to exercise the round-trip; kernel tasks + SCHED itself stay
154	  kernel-scheduled (avoids a scheduler scheduling itself).
155	- **Files**: `kernel/src/proc/{proc_struct,sched}.rs`, `kernel/src/clock.rs`,
156	  new `kernel/src/system/do_schedule.rs` (replaces the two stubs), new
157	  `kernel/src/ipc/` no-quantum send helper, `kernel/src/system/mod.rs`,
158	  `kernel/src/proc/table.rs`, `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`; new
159	  `servers/sched/{…}`.
160	- **Verify**: trace shows a SCHED-scheduled proc running out of quantum →
161	  `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM` to SCHED → `SYS_SCHEDULE` re-admits it; kernel-
162	  scheduled procs (VM/DS/VFS) still round-robin; no starvation/panic over 8 s.
163	
164	### Slice 4.4 — RS (reincarnation server) + real `SYS_SETALARM`
165	
166	- **`SYS_SETALARM` (kernel)**: per-proc one-shot timer hung off the existing
167	  clock tick; fires a notification to the owner at expiry. (Currently an ENOSYS
168	  stub.) Needed by RS for periodic health checks and later by PM for `alarm()`.
169	- **RS server** (`servers/rs`, server-rt based, already `ROOT_SYS_PROC` +
170	  `sig_mgr` for all): at `init_fresh`, record the boot servers (from DS or a
171	  static list); arm a periodic `SYS_SETALARM`; on each alarm, ping/heartbeat the
172	  servers and log liveness. Restart-on-crash is **minimal** in Phase 4 (detect a
173	  dead/unresponsive server and log; full reload via the boot archive can reuse
174	  exec's loader but is a stretch goal — keep the slice bounded).
175	- **Files**: new `kernel/src/system/do_setalarm.rs`, `kernel/src/clock.rs`
176	  (alarm queue), `kernel/src/system/mod.rs`, `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs` (RS_*
177	  + alarm notify); new `servers/rs/{…}`.
178	- **Verify**: trace shows RS arming an alarm, the alarm firing periodically, and
179	  RS heartbeating DS/VM/SCHED/VFS; everything else unchanged.
180	
181	### Slice 4.5 — PM part A: process table + getpid + `SYS_PRIVCTL` + minimal signals
182	
183	Stand up PM with everything *except* fork/exec, so the heavy slice (4.6) is just
184	the lifecycle.
185	- **PM server** (`servers/pm`, server-rt based): static `mproc` table
186	  (`UnsafeCell` newtype, one entry per proc slot). Handle `getpid`/`getppid`
187	  (trivial), and the signal scaffolding.
188	- **`SYS_PRIVCTL` (kernel)**: minimal — set/initialize a target proc's priv slot
189	  (used by PM/RS to privilege a new process; the fork path in 4.6 leans on it).
190	- **Minimal signals**: a kill path with default action = terminate. PM receives
191	  `kill(pid, sig)`, looks up the target, and asks the kernel to deliver/terminate
192	  (a small `SYS_` mechanism — fold into the exit/teardown path rather than a full
193	  POSIX signal machine; handlers/sigaction/masks are out of Phase 4 scope). This
194	  finally gives the Phase-3 VM "SIGSEGV leaves faulter blocked" path a real
195	  consequence (PM terminates the faulter).
196	- **Files**: new `servers/pm/{…}`, new `servers/pm/src/{table,signal}.rs`; new
197	  `kernel/src/system/do_privctl.rs`, `kernel/src/system/mod.rs`,
198	  `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs` (PM_* + signal numbers).
199	- **Verify**: a test stub calls `getpid` via PM and gets a sane pid; a deliberate
200	  out-of-region fault now ends in PM terminating the faulter (not a silent hang);
201	  other servers unchanged.
202	
203	### Slice 4.6 — PM part B: fork + exit + wait
204	
205	The lifecycle core.
206	- **`SYS_FORK` (kernel)**: allocate a free proc slot, copy the parent's register
207	  frame, derive the child endpoint (bump generation), allocate an ASID, build the
208	  child's address space by walking the parent's TTBR0 and copying each mapped
209	  user page into a fresh frame via HHDM (eager copy; CoW deferred). The child
210	  uses the **shared USER priv** (or a derived system priv if the parent is a
211	  system proc). Child returns 0, parent returns child pid.
212	- **`SYS_EXIT` (kernel)**: tear down the address space (free frames + page-table
213	  tree via `AddrSpace::destroy`), free the proc slot, hand status to PM.
214	- **`VM_FORK` (VM request)**: VM clones the parent's `ClientRegions` into the
215	  child's region slot so the child's later faults resolve against correct
216	  regions.
217	- **PM**: owns the process tree — `fork` (call `SYS_FORK`, register child in
218	  `mproc`, `VM_FORK` to VM, `SCHEDULING_START` to SCHED), `exit` (zombie, signal
219	  parent via SIGCHLD), `wait`/`waitpid` (reap zombies, return status).
220	- **Files**: new `kernel/src/system/{do_fork,do_exit}.rs`,
221	  `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/addrspace.rs` (AS-copy helper),
222	  `kernel/src/system/mod.rs`, `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`; `servers/pm/src/*`;
223	  `servers/vm/src/{main,region}.rs` (VM_FORK).
224	- **Verify**: a test stub (or an init-lite) forks; child runs in its own AS,
225	  exits; parent `wait`s and reaps the correct status. Trace shows
226	  `SYS_FORK`/`VM_FORK`/`SCHEDULING_START`/`SYS_EXIT` round-trips, all `result=0`.
227	
228	### Slice 4.7 — exec: `SYS_EXEC` + PM exec of a boot-embedded binary
229	
230	- **`SYS_EXEC` (kernel)**: replace the caller's address space with a freshly
231	  loaded ELF — build a new `AddrSpace`, `boot_image::elf::load_into` the named
232	  module (resolved via 4.2's `module_by_name`), set up a minimal initial user
233	  stack (argv/env minimal/empty), swap `ttbr0_pa` + `destroy` the old AS, set
234	  `elr_el1` = entry / `sp_el0` = stack top.
235	- **PM**: `execve(name)` selects the embedded binary and issues `SYS_EXEC`. (The
236	  binary "store" is the boot archive; Phase 5 swaps this for a VFS file read with
237	  no PM/kernel change.)
238	- **Embedded demo binary**: a tiny freestanding `servers/` (or `userland/`)
239	  "worker" ELF packed into the archive that init's children exec.
240	- **Files**: new `kernel/src/system/do_exec.rs`, `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/`
241	  (initial-stack setup), `kernel/src/system/mod.rs`, `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs`;
242	  `servers/pm/src/*`; `kernel/build.rs` + archive (pack the worker ELF).
243	- **Verify**: a stub forks a child; the child execs the embedded worker; the
244	  worker runs (distinct trace) and exits; parent reaps. `result=0` throughout.
245	
246	### Slice 4.8 — init (PID 1) + Phase 4 wrap-up + docs
247	
248	The capstone.
249	- **init** (`userland/init`, freestanding Rust ELF loaded into `INIT_PROC_NR=10`
250	  by the boot archive — PM does not need to create it; the kernel boots it like a
251	  server): as PID 1, fork children that exec the embedded worker, `wait` for
252	  them, respawn in a loop; optionally send/receive a signal to exercise the PM
253	  signal path.
254	- **Full boot chain** DS → RS → SCHED → VFS → PM → init completes and is visible
255	  in one 8 s trace.
256	- **Retire/demote** stubs A/B/C/D now that init + real procs are the live
257	  exercise (keep one as a minimal IPC regression if cheap; document the choice).
258	- **Docs**: real mdBook *Servers* chapter (`book/src/servers/overview.md`) —
259	  SEF, the boot sequence, DS/RS/SCHED/VFS/PM roles, fork/exec/exit/wait, the
260	  delegatable scheduler. `CLAUDE.md`: Phase-4 conventions (SEF callback shape,
261	  shared USER priv, delegatable-scheduler invariant, fork AS-copy ownership,
262	  exec-from-archive). Flip every Phase-4 slice marker and write the Phase-4-
263	  complete aggregate paragraph mirroring the Phase 2/3 prose.
264	- **Verify**: 8 s trace shows the full boot chain, init forking/exec/waiting in a
265	  loop, RS heartbeats, SCHED-driven quantum refresh, no panic / no
266	  `el0_sync_unexpected` / zero nonzero results. **Phase 4 milestone reached.**
267	
268	---
269	
270	## Aggregate scope (Phase 4 as a whole)
271	
272	- `minix-ipc`: NOTIFY + SENDNB primitives.
273	- `server-rt`: SEF startup + receive loop + init/signal callbacks (minimal subset).
274	- Multi-module boot-image archive (MXBI) + generalized server loader.
275	- Kernel calls made real: `SYS_FORK`, `SYS_EXEC`, `SYS_EXIT`, `SYS_PRIVCTL`,
276	  `SYS_SCHEDULE`, `SYS_SETALARM`, plus a new `SYS_SCHEDCTL` (and a minimal signal
277	  mechanism). Delegatable scheduler (`Proc::scheduler`).
278	- Servers: DS (key/value + endpoint registry), SCHED (real user-space policy),
279	  RS (heartbeat monitor), VFS (skeletal boot), PM (process table, fork, exec,
280	  exit, wait, getpid, minimal signals) — all static-allocation, no heap.
281	- init (PID 1) forking/exec/waiting; embedded "worker" binary as exec target.
282	- **Milestone:** Full server boot sequence completes; init process starts.
283	
284	---
285	
286	## Critical files & reuse (don't reinvent)
287	
288	- **`servers/vm/`** — the working server template: `_start` shim + `cfg`-gating
289	  (`servers/vm/src/main.rs:55-64`), the SENDREC-to-SYSTEM pattern, `user.ld`
290	  (base `0x0010_0000`), the `rd_u64`/`wr_i32`/`wr_u64` payload accessors. Every
291	  Phase-4 server clones this shape.
292	- **`kernel/build.rs::build_vm_server`** — the nested-cargo, separate
293	  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `CARGO_ENCODED_RUSTFLAGS` linker-override, `rustc-env` ELF-
294	  path pattern. Generalize, don't rewrite.
295	- **`kernel/src/arch/aarch64/userland.rs::vm_bootstrap`** — the load-an-ELF-into-
296	  a-proc-slot sequence (`AddrSpace::new` → `elf::load_into` → stack frame →
297	  set regs → `forget(aspace)` → `enqueue`). Generalize to `load_boot_server`.
298	- **`kernel/src/proc/table.rs`** — `IMAGE` already lists every server with
299	  correct priv flags/priority/quantum, and `init_boot_image` already fills
300	  `ipc_to`/`k_call_mask` for `SRV_T`. Boot servers need **no new priv wiring** —
301	  just an ELF + `load_boot_server`. Widen `fill_bits(k_call_mask, …)` only when
302	  the kernel-call count grows (4.3).
303	- **`kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs::load_into`** — reused unchanged by the
304	  multi-module loader (4.2) and by `SYS_EXEC` (4.7).
305	- **`kernel/src/system/do_vmctl.rs`** — the model for new kernel-call handlers
306	  that act on a target proc named in the message (the `&mut [Proc]` slice +
307	  `caller_nr` form routed specially in `kernel_call_dispatch`); `SYS_FORK`/
308	  `SYS_EXEC`/`SYS_SCHEDULE`/`SYS_SCHEDCTL` follow it.
309	- **`kernel/src/proc/sched.rs::{rts_set,rts_unset}`** — capture-then-borrow-end
310	  NLL helpers for every RTS↔run-queue transition (no-quantum delegation, fork
311	  child admission, exit teardown).
312	- **`kernel/src/ipc/send.rs::mini_pf_send`** — the kernel-originated SEND
313	  template for the `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM`-to-scheduler message (4.3).
314	- **`kernel-shared/src/com.rs`** — `PM/VFS/RS/DS/SCHED/INIT_PROC_NR` and
315	  `boot_endpoint` already defined; Phase 4 only adds call numbers in `callnr.rs`
316	  (suggest per-server bases consistent with `VM_RQ_BASE = 0xC00`).
317	
318	---
319	
320	## Verification (per slice — same QEMU rhythm as Phases 2–3)
321	
322	```sh
323	cargo kernel-aarch64
324	timeout 8 cargo run -p minixrs-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release \
325	  > /tmp/phase4.log 2>&1
326	# grep -a the log; the tick bytes interleave with traces.
327	```
328	
329	Each slice's milestone is a grep pattern in `/tmp/phase4.log` (whoami/SEF
330	startup line per server; DS publish/retrieve; SCHED no-quantum round-trip; RS
331	heartbeat; PM getpid/fork/exit/wait; exec worker run; init loop). Every slice
332	must keep the **earlier-phase regressions** green: A/B ping-pong, C
333	`SYS_GETINFO`, VM fault resolution, clock-tick interleaving — until the relevant
334	stub is intentionally retired in 4.8. Phase 4 ships when one 8 s trace shows the
335	full DS→RS→SCHED→VFS→PM→init boot chain with init forking/exec/waiting, no panic,
336	no `el0_sync_unexpected`, zero nonzero results. Grow `kernel-shared`,
337	`server-rt`, and per-server host tests alongside new types every slice
338	(`cargo test -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-server-rt …` stays green).
339	
340	---
341	
342	## This session's deliverable (decomposition only)
343	
344	Per the user's choice, this session produces the decomposition, not slice 4.1.
345	After plan approval:
346	
347	1. **Edit `docs/plan.md`**: replace the flat Phase 4 bullet list (currently
348	   ~lines 795–803) with the sliced breakdown above, in the established slice-
349	   tracker prose style — an intro paragraph (8 slices, decomposition tracked in
350	   this plan file, milestone satisfied at 4.8), each slice as a bullet with a
351	   `◀ next` marker on **4.1** and the rest unmarked (unstarted), then the
352	   "Aggregate scope (Phase 4 as a whole)" block and the milestone line. Mirror
353	   exactly how Phase 2 and Phase 3 are presented in `docs/plan.md` today.
354	2. Leave this file as the detailed execution plan for the per-slice sessions that
355	   follow (one branch/PR per slice, `feature/phase-4-N-…`).
356	
357	No code, server crates, kernel calls, or build changes land this session.
