# Slice 4.4 — RS (reincarnation server) + real `SYS_SETALARM`

## Context

minix.rs is a 64-bit Rust reimplementation of MINIX 3. Phases 1–3 and slices
4.1–4.3 are shipped: the kernel does IPC, GICv3+timer scheduling (delegatable as
of 4.3), per-proc TTBR0/ASID address spaces, page-fault→VM routing, and real
`SYS_GETINFO`/`SYS_VMCTL`/`SYS_SCHEDULE`/`SYS_SCHEDCTL`. Four servers boot through
the SEF framework (`server-rt`) off a multi-module MXBI archive: **VM, DS, VFS,
SCHED**. Four hand-coded EL0 stubs (A/B/C/D) remain as regression coverage.

**This slice** (the `◀ next` marker in `docs/plan.md`) delivers two things:

1. **Real `SYS_SETALARM`** — currently an `ENOSYS` stub. A per-proc one-shot timer
   hung off the existing 100 Hz clock tick; on expiry the kernel delivers a
   `NOTIFY` to the alarm's owner. This is the first real consumer of the
   `clock::uptime()` counter that's been waiting since slice 2.4.
2. **RS, the reincarnation server** (`servers/rs`) — already listed in the boot
   `IMAGE` with `ROOT_SYS_PROC` privileges but with no ELF, so it sits blocked on
   `RTS_NO_PRIV`. RS boots through SEF, publishes to DS, arms a periodic alarm via
   `SYS_SETALARM`, and on each fire heartbeats (pings) the other boot servers and
   tracks their liveness. Restart-on-crash is **minimal** (detect-and-record only;
   no live reload) — kept bounded per the Phase-4 decomposition.

Why now: RS is the alarm mechanism's first user and the next server in the
DS→RS→SCHED→VFS→PM→init boot chain. `SYS_SETALARM` is also needed later by PM for
`alarm()` and by SCHED for priority aging (4.5+). The intended outcome: an 8–10 s
QEMU trace shows RS arming an alarm, the alarm firing periodically, and RS
heartbeating DS/VM/SCHED/VFS — with every earlier-phase regression (A↔B
ping-pong, stub C `SYS_GETINFO`, stub D faults→VM, SCHED delegation) intact.

**Key facts established during exploration (no new priv wiring needed):**
- RS is `IMAGE` slot 7 / priv id 7, `trap_mask = SRV_T` (all IPC primitives incl.
  NOTIFY), `priv_flags = SYS_PROC | PREEMPTIBLE | ROOT_SYS_PROC`, priority `RS_Q=4`.
  `init_boot_image` already fills RS's `ipc_to` over `[0, 16)` and its
  `k_call_mask` to `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` — so RS can already `SYS_SETALARM` and
  `ipc_notify` every boot server. RS just needs an ELF + the existing
  `load_boot_server` path (`table.rs:178`, `userland.rs` load loop).
- `CLOCK` is a kernel task with a real priv slot (id 2, `proc_nr = Some(CLOCK)`),
  so it can be the notification source. But `CLOCK.ipc_to` is empty (only `SRV_T`
  slots get it filled), so alarm delivery must **not** route through `mini_notify`
  (its `ipc_to` check would deny CLOCK) — it needs a dedicated kernel-originated
  helper that skips the permission check, exactly like
  `mini_pf_send`/`mini_sched_no_quantum_send` do.
- `SRV_T = !0` → RS can `ipc_notify` peers; `server-rt`'s `sef.receive` already
  acks RS pings via `ipc_notify(RS)` (`sef.rs`), so the ping/ack protocol exists.

**Decision (confirmed with user):** RS monitors a **static boot-endpoint list**
(DS/VM/SCHED/VFS via `boot_endpoint(*_PROC_NR)`) — bounded, no init-ordering
hazard. DS-driven discovery is a later enhancement.

---

## Design

### Alarm mechanism (kernel)

- **Source of truth:** a new per-proc field `Proc::alarm_at: u64` — absolute
  uptime tick at which to fire; `0` = disarmed.
- **Fast-path gate:** a `static EARLIEST_ALARM: AtomicU64` in `clock.rs`
  (`0` = no alarm armed). The common tick stays O(1): only when
  `EARLIEST_ALARM != 0 && now >= EARLIEST_ALARM` does the kernel do the O(N)
  proc-table scan to fire expired alarms and recompute the next-earliest. Fires
  are rare (RS arms ~1/s), so the rare scan is fine. (The kernel is single-CPU /
  DAIF-masked, so SVC-context `do_setalarm` and IRQ-context `tick` are mutually
  exclusive — plain atomic load/store, no CAS.)
- **Delivery = NOTIFY from CLOCK.** On expiry the kernel synthesizes a
  `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` with `m_source = boot_endpoint(CLOCK)` into the owner. This
  reuses existing constants (`NOTIFY_MESSAGE`, `CLOCK`) — **no new kernel-shared
  constants needed**. Immediate delivery if the owner is `RECEIVE`-blocked from
  CLOCK/ANY; otherwise the deferred `notify_pending` path (set CLOCK's priv-bit;
  `mini_receive::take_pending_notification` already maps the bit → CLOCK endpoint
  on the owner's next RECEIVE). RS is receive-blocked at fire time by
  construction, so it normally takes the immediate path; the deferred path is
  there for robustness (owner busy at the tick).
- **Why NOTIFY/CLOCK won't be misclassified:** `server-rt::classify` only
  intercepts `NOTIFY` from **RS** (ping), `SEF_SIGNAL` from PM/RS, `SEF_INIT` from
  RS. A `NOTIFY` from CLOCK is `Application`, so RS's loop sees it and keys on
  `m_source == boot_endpoint(CLOCK)`. Only RS arms an alarm this slice, so no
  other server ever receives a CLOCK notify.

### RS server (user-space, EL0, SEF-based)

RS clones the SCHED server's shape (`servers/sched/`): `_start` shim, `sef_startup`
+ `sef.receive` loop, `wr_i32`/`rd_i32` payload helpers, freestanding panic
handler, all `not(test)`/`target_os="none"`-gated so `cargo check --workspace` and
host `cargo test` still work. Pure liveness bookkeeping lives in a separate
`monitor.rs` (host-tested, like `sched/policy.rs` and `ds/registry.rs`); the IPC
glue in `main.rs` is coverage-excluded by the existing `servers/**/src/main.rs`
sonar glob.

RS protocol (all observable kernel-side; EL0 has no console):
- **init_fresh:** `sef_publish_to_ds(name)` (RS publishes its own endpoint).
- **startup (before loop):** arm the first alarm — `SYS_SETALARM(ALARM_PERIOD)`
  SENDREC to SYSTEM. `ALARM_PERIOD = 100` ticks (≈1 s at 100 Hz → ~8–10 fires in
  an 8 s run).

  - `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` from `boot_endpoint(CLOCK)` → **alarm tick**:
    `monitor::sweep()` (record any peer that missed last round — detect-only, no
    restart), reset the round, `ipc_notify` each monitored peer, then re-arm
    `SYS_SETALARM(ALARM_PERIOD)` (one-shot → periodic by re-arming).
  - `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` from a monitored peer → **heartbeat ack**:
    `monitor::mark_alive(m_source)`.
  - anything else → drop.

`monitor.rs` (pure, host-tested): static `[Server; N]` `UnsafeCell` newtype
(`unsafe impl Sync`, single-threaded EL0) holding `{endpoint, alive_this_round,
missed_rounds}`. Pure fns: `init(endpoints)`, `mark_alive(e)`, `sweep()` (bump
`missed_rounds` for peers not seen, clear flags, return count/over-threshold list
for the detect path), tested with the standard host-test pattern.

## Approved Plan (edited by user):
# Slice 4.4 — RS (reincarnation server) + real `SYS_SETALARM`

## Context

minix.rs is a 64-bit Rust reimplementation of MINIX 3. Phases 1–3 and slices
4.1–4.3 are shipped: the kernel does IPC, GICv3+timer scheduling (delegatable as
of 4.3), per-proc TTBR0/ASID address spaces, page-fault→VM routing, and real
`SYS_GETINFO`/`SYS_VMCTL`/`SYS_SCHEDULE`/`SYS_SCHEDCTL`. Four servers boot through
the SEF framework (`server-rt`) off a multi-module MXBI archive: **VM, DS, VFS,
SCHED**. Four hand-coded EL0 stubs (A/B/C/D) remain as regression coverage.

**This slice** (the `◀ next` marker in `docs/plan.md`) delivers two things:

1. **Real `SYS_SETALARM`** — currently an `ENOSYS` stub. A per-proc one-shot timer
   hung off the existing 100 Hz clock tick; on expiry the kernel delivers a
   `NOTIFY` to the alarm's owner. This is the first real consumer of the
   `clock::uptime()` counter that's been waiting since slice 2.4.
2. **RS, the reincarnation server** (`servers/rs`) — already listed in the boot
   `IMAGE` with `ROOT_SYS_PROC` privileges but with no ELF, so it sits blocked on
   `RTS_NO_PRIV`. RS boots through SEF, publishes to DS, arms a periodic alarm via
   `SYS_SETALARM`, and on each fire heartbeats (pings) the other boot servers and
   tracks their liveness. Restart-on-crash is **minimal** (detect-and-record only;
   no live reload) — kept bounded per the Phase-4 decomposition.

Why now: RS is the alarm mechanism's first user and the next server in the
DS→RS→SCHED→VFS→PM→init boot chain. `SYS_SETALARM` is also needed later by PM for
`alarm()` and by SCHED for priority aging (4.5+). The intended outcome: an 8–10 s
QEMU trace shows RS arming an alarm, the alarm firing periodically, and RS
heartbeating DS/VM/SCHED/VFS — with every earlier-phase regression (A↔B
ping-pong, stub C `SYS_GETINFO`, stub D faults→VM, SCHED delegation) intact.

**Key facts established during exploration (no new priv wiring needed):**
- RS is `IMAGE` slot 7 / priv id 7, `trap_mask = SRV_T` (all IPC primitives incl.
  NOTIFY), `priv_flags = SYS_PROC | PREEMPTIBLE | ROOT_SYS_PROC`, priority `RS_Q=4`.
  `init_boot_image` already fills RS's `ipc_to` over `[0, 16)` and its
  `k_call_mask` to `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` — so RS can already `SYS_SETALARM` and
  `ipc_notify` every boot server. RS just needs an ELF + the existing
  `load_boot_server` path (`table.rs:178`, `userland.rs` load loop).
- `CLOCK` is a kernel task with a real priv slot (id 2, `proc_nr = Some(CLOCK)`),
  so it can be the notification source. But `CLOCK.ipc_to` is empty (only `SRV_T`
  slots get it filled), so alarm delivery must **not** route through `mini_notify`
  (its `ipc_to` check would deny CLOCK) — it needs a dedicated kernel-originated
  helper that skips the permission check, exactly like
  `mini_pf_send`/`mini_sched_no_quantum_send` do.
- `SRV_T = !0` → RS can `ipc_notify` peers; `server-rt`'s `sef.receive` already
  acks RS pings via `ipc_notify(RS)` (`sef.rs`), so the ping/ack protocol exists.

**Decision (confirmed with user):** RS monitors a **static boot-endpoint list**
(DS/VM/SCHED/VFS via `boot_endpoint(*_PROC_NR)`) — bounded, no init-ordering
hazard. DS-driven discovery is a later enhancement.

---

## Design

### Alarm mechanism (kernel)

- **Source of truth:** a new per-proc field `Proc::alarm_at: u64` — absolute
  uptime tick at which to fire; `0` = disarmed.
- **Fast-path gate:** a `static EARLIEST_ALARM: AtomicU64` in `clock.rs`
  (`0` = no alarm armed). The common tick stays O(1): only when
  `EARLIEST_ALARM != 0 && now >= EARLIEST_ALARM` does the kernel do the O(N)
  proc-table scan to fire expired alarms and recompute the next-earliest. Fires
  are rare (RS arms ~1/s), so the rare scan is fine. (The kernel is single-CPU /
  DAIF-masked, so SVC-context `do_setalarm` and IRQ-context `tick` are mutually
  exclusive — plain atomic load/store, no CAS.)
- **Delivery = NOTIFY from CLOCK.** On expiry the kernel synthesizes a
  `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` with `m_source = boot_endpoint(CLOCK)` into the owner. This
  reuses existing constants (`NOTIFY_MESSAGE`, `CLOCK`) — **no new kernel-shared
  constants needed**. Immediate delivery if the owner is `RECEIVE`-blocked from
  CLOCK/ANY; otherwise the deferred `notify_pending` path (set CLOCK's priv-bit;
  `mini_receive::take_pending_notification` already maps the bit → CLOCK endpoint
  on the owner's next RECEIVE). RS is receive-blocked at fire time by
  construction, so it normally takes the immediate path; the deferred path is
  there for robustness (owner busy at the tick).
- **Why NOTIFY/CLOCK won't be misclassified:** `server-rt::classify` only
  intercepts `NOTIFY` from **RS** (ping), `SEF_SIGNAL` from PM/RS, `SEF_INIT` from
  RS. A `NOTIFY` from CLOCK is `Application`, so RS's loop sees it and keys on
  `m_source == boot_endpoint(CLOCK)`. Only RS arms an alarm this slice, so no
  other server ever receives a CLOCK notify.

### RS server (user-space, EL0, SEF-based)

RS clones the SCHED server's shape (`servers/sched/`): `_start` shim, `sef_startup`
+ `sef.receive` loop, `wr_i32`/`rd_i32` payload helpers, freestanding panic
handler, all `not(test)`/`target_os="none"`-gated so `cargo check --workspace` and
host `cargo test` still work. Pure liveness bookkeeping lives in a separate
`monitor.rs` (host-tested, like `sched/policy.rs` and `ds/registry.rs`); the IPC
glue in `main.rs` is coverage-excluded by the existing `servers/**/src/main.rs`
sonar glob.

RS protocol (all observable kernel-side; EL0 has no console):
- **init_fresh:** `sef_publish_to_ds(name)` (RS publishes its own endpoint).
- **startup (before loop):** arm the first alarm — `SYS_SETALARM(ALARM_PERIOD)`
  SENDREC to SYSTEM. `ALARM_PERIOD = 100` ticks (≈1 s at 100 Hz → ~8–10 fires in
  an 8 s run).
- **loop dispatch on `(m_type, m_source)`:**
  - `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` from `boot_endpoint(CLOCK)` → **alarm tick**:
    `monitor::sweep()` (record any peer that missed last round — detect-only, no
    restart), reset the round, `ipc_notify` each monitored peer, then re-arm
    `SYS_SETALARM(ALARM_PERIOD)` (one-shot → periodic by re-arming).
  - `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` from a monitored peer → **heartbeat ack**:
    `monitor::mark_alive(m_source)`.
  - anything else → drop.

`monitor.rs` (pure, host-tested): static `[Server; N]` `UnsafeCell` newtype
(`unsafe impl Sync`, single-threaded EL0) holding `{endpoint, alive_this_round,
missed_rounds}`. Pure fns: `init(endpoints)`, `mark_alive(e)`, `sweep()` (bump
`missed_rounds` for peers not seen, clear flags, return count/over-threshold list
for the detect path), tested with the standard host-test pattern.

---

## Implementation

### Kernel

1. **`kernel/src/proc/proc_struct.rs`** — add `pub alarm_at: u64` in a new
   `// ----- Timer / alarm state -----` block (place after `sys_time`, before the
   IPC-state block, or alongside scheduling state). Set `alarm_at: 0` in
   `Proc::EMPTY`.

2. **`kernel/src/clock.rs`** —
   - Drop the `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `uptime()` (now a real consumer).
   - Add `static EARLIEST_ALARM: AtomicU64` (0 = none) plus:
     - `pub fn arm_alarm(at: u64)` — set `EARLIEST_ALARM = min(current,at)` treating
       0 as "none" (load + conditional store; single-threaded).
     - `pub fn set_earliest_alarm(at: u64)` — unconditional store (called by
       `fire_expired_alarms` with the recomputed minimum, 0 if none left).
   - In `tick()`, right after `UPTIME.fetch_add`, **before** `current_proc_mut()`:
     `let now = … ; if EARLIEST_ALARM != 0 && now >= earliest { ipc::fire_expired_alarms(now); }`.
     The borrow inside `fire_expired_alarms` ends on return (NLL), so it can't
     alias the later `current_proc_mut()` borrow.

3. **`kernel/src/ipc/mod.rs`** — add
   `pub fn fire_expired_alarms(now: u64)` (a kernel-originated-delivery wrapper
   beside `send_pagefault_to_vm` / `send_no_quantum`): materialize
   `proc_table_mut_slice()` + `priv_table_mut_slice()`, scan all `N_PROC_SLOTS`
   for `alarm_at != 0`; for each expired (`alarm_at <= now`): clear `alarm_at`,
   call `notify::deliver_alarm(proc_table, priv_table, owner_idx)`, and emit an
   `[alarm N]` trace (head carve-out + modulo-sampled, mirroring `TRACE_HEAD` /
   `NOQ_TRACE_HEAD` so RS's periodic fire is visible despite stub C's flood).
   Track the min of still-armed `alarm_at` and call
   `clock::set_earliest_alarm(min_or_0)`.

4. **`kernel/src/ipc/notify.rs`** — add
   `pub(crate) fn deliver_alarm(proc_table, priv_table, owner_idx)`: look up
   CLOCK's `(priv_id, endpoint)` via `proc_table[proc_index(CLOCK)]`. If
   `will_receive(owner, clock_e) && owner.misc_flags & MF_REPLY_PEND == 0`:
   `build_notify_message(owner, clock_e)` + `MF_DELIVERMSG` +
   `rts_unset(RTS_RECEIVING)`. Else `set_sys_bit(priv_table[owner_priv].notify_pending, clock_priv_id)`.
   **No `ipc_to` check** (kernel-originated). Reuses the existing pub(crate)
   `will_receive` / `build_notify_message`.

5. **`kernel/src/system/do_setalarm.rs`** (new) — real handler replacing the
   stub. Caller-local signature `(caller: &mut Proc, _caller_priv: &Priv, msg: &mut Message) -> i32`.
   Read `delta: u64` from `payload[0..8]` (`from_ne_bytes`). Compute
   `prev_remaining = caller.alarm_at.saturating_sub(now)` (0 if disarmed). If
   `delta == 0` → disarm (`alarm_at = 0`); else `alarm_at = now + delta` and
   `clock::arm_alarm(alarm_at)`. Write `prev_remaining` into `payload[0..8]` (MINIX
   returns the previous time-left; RS ignores it). Return `OK`.

6. **`kernel/src/system/mod.rs`** — add `mod do_setalarm;`; change the
   `SYS_SETALARM` arm in `dispatch_caller_local` from `stubs::do_setalarm(...)` to
   `do_setalarm::do_setalarm(...)`. `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` stays **15** — the
   `const _: () = assert!(NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 == 15, …)` is unchanged (no new
   call number). Remove the `enosys_stub!(do_setalarm)` line from
   `kernel/src/system/stubs.rs`.

### Server

7. **`servers/rs/Cargo.toml`** (new) — mirror `servers/sched/Cargo.toml` (deps:
   `minixrs-kernel-shared`, `minixrs-ipc`, `minixrs-server-rt`; `publish = false`).

8. **`servers/rs/user.ld`** (new) — copy `servers/sched/user.ld` verbatim (base
   `0x0010_0000`, page-aligned PT_LOADs).

9. **`servers/rs/src/main.rs`** (new) — SEF loop per the protocol above
   (clone of `servers/sched/src/main.rs` structure). Monitored set =
   `[boot_endpoint(DS_PROC_NR), boot_endpoint(VM_PROC_NR), boot_endpoint(SCHED_PROC_NR), boot_endpoint(VFS_PROC_NR)]`.

10. **`servers/rs/src/monitor.rs`** (new) — pure liveness table + host tests
    (pattern: `servers/sched/src/policy.rs`).

### Build + boot

11. **`kernel/build.rs`** — append `("minixrs-rs", workspace.join("servers/rs"), 2)`
    to the `servers` array (length `4 → 5`; proc_nr 2 = `RS_PROC_NR`). Add
    `servers/rs/{src,user.ld,Cargo.toml}` to the rerun-if-changed list. No loader
    change — `userland.rs`'s `BootImage::iter()` loop + `init_boot_image` priv fill
    pick RS up automatically.

### Docs / config

12. **`docs/plan.md`** (as part of the PR): reconcile the **stale 4.3 marker**
    `◀ ready (branch feature/phase-4-3-sched, pending merge)` → `✓ shipped (PR #25,
    merged 2026-06-27)`; flip **4.4** `◀ next` → `◀ ready (branch
    feature/phase-4-4-rs-setalarm, pending merge)` with a shipped-style paragraph;
    slide `◀ next` to **4.5**.
13. **kernel-shared / sonar:** **no changes expected.** No new call numbers or
    RS request range (RS uses NOTIFY; `RS_RQ_BASE` deferred to 4.5 when PM drives
    RS). `servers/rs/src/main.rs` is auto-covered by the existing
    `servers/**/src/main.rs` sonar glob; `monitor.rs` is host-tested. Run the
    pre-PR `revise-claude-md` to capture any 4.4 conventions worth recording
    (alarm mechanism, RS shape).

---

## Critical files & reuse (don't reinvent)

- `servers/sched/src/{main.rs,policy.rs}`, `servers/sched/{Cargo.toml,user.ld}` —
  the server template RS clones.
- `kernel/src/ipc/notify.rs` — `will_receive`, `build_notify_message` (reuse for
  `deliver_alarm`); `take_pending_notification` in `receive.rs` already drains the
  deferred path → CLOCK endpoint.

- `kernel/src/system/do_schedule.rs` (caller-local + payload accessors) /

- `kernel/src/proc/table.rs` (`IMAGE` RS entry + `init_boot_image`) — confirms **no

glue in `main.rs` is coverage-excluded by the existing `servers/**/src/main.rs`
sonar glob.

RS protocol (all observable kernel-side; EL0 has no console):
- **init_fresh:** `sef_publish_to_ds(name)` (RS publishes its own endpoint).
- **startup (before loop):** arm the first alarm — `SYS_SETALARM(ALARM_PERIOD)`
  SENDREC to SYSTEM. `ALARM_PERIOD = 100` ticks (≈1 s at 100 Hz → ~8–10 fires in
  an 8 s run).
- **loop dispatch on `(m_type, m_source)`:**
  - `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` from `boot_endpoint(CLOCK)` → **alarm tick**:
    `monitor::sweep()` (record any peer that missed last round — detect-only, no
    restart), reset the round, `ipc_notify` each monitored peer, then re-arm
    `SYS_SETALARM(ALARM_PERIOD)` (one-shot → periodic by re-arming).
  - `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` from a monitored peer → **heartbeat ack**:
    `monitor::mark_alive(m_source)`.
  - anything else → drop.

`monitor.rs` (pure, host-tested): static `[Server; N]` `UnsafeCell` newtype
(`unsafe impl Sync`, single-threaded EL0) holding `{endpoint, alive_this_round,
missed_rounds}`. Pure fns: `init(endpoints)`, `mark_alive(e)`, `sweep()` (bump
`missed_rounds` for peers not seen, clear flags, return count/over-threshold list
for the detect path), tested with the standard host-test pattern.

---

## Implementation

### Kernel

1. **`kernel/src/proc/proc_struct.rs`** — add `pub alarm_at: u64` in a new
   `// ----- Timer / alarm state -----` block (place after `sys_time`, before the
   IPC-state block, or alongside scheduling state). Set `alarm_at: 0` in
   `Proc::EMPTY`.

2. **`kernel/src/clock.rs`** —
   - Drop the `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `uptime()` (now a real consumer).
   - Add `static EARLIEST_ALARM: AtomicU64` (0 = none) plus:
     - `pub fn arm_alarm(at: u64)` — set `EARLIEST_ALARM = min(current,at)` treating
       0 as "none" (load + conditional store; single-threaded).
     - `pub fn set_earliest_alarm(at: u64)` — unconditional store (called by
       `fire_expired_alarms` with the recomputed minimum, 0 if none left).
   - In `tick()`, right after `UPTIME.fetch_add`, **before** `current_proc_mut()`:
     `let now = … ; if EARLIEST_ALARM != 0 && now >= earliest { ipc::fire_expired_alarms(now); }`.
     The borrow inside `fire_expired_alarms` ends on return (NLL), so it can't
     alias the later `current_proc_mut()` borrow.

3. **`kernel/src/ipc/mod.rs`** — add
   `pub fn fire_expired_alarms(now: u64)` (a kernel-originated-delivery wrapper
   beside `send_pagefault_to_vm` / `send_no_quantum`): materialize
   `proc_table_mut_slice()` + `priv_table_mut_slice()`, scan all `N_PROC_SLOTS`
   for `alarm_at != 0`; for each expired (`alarm_at <= now`): clear `alarm_at`,
   call `notify::deliver_alarm(proc_table, priv_table, owner_idx)`, and emit an
   `[alarm N]` trace (head carve-out + modulo-sampled, mirroring `TRACE_HEAD` /
   `NOQ_TRACE_HEAD` so RS's periodic fire is visible despite stub C's flood).
   Track the min of still-armed `alarm_at` and call
   `clock::set_earliest_alarm(min_or_0)`.

4. **`kernel/src/ipc/notify.rs`** — add
   `pub(crate) fn deliver_alarm(proc_table, priv_table, owner_idx)`: look up
   CLOCK's `(priv_id, endpoint)` via `proc_table[proc_index(CLOCK)]`. If
   `will_receive(owner, clock_e) && owner.misc_flags & MF_REPLY_PEND == 0`:
   `build_notify_message(owner, clock_e)` + `MF_DELIVERMSG` +
   `rts_unset(RTS_RECEIVING)`. Else `set_sys_bit(priv_table[owner_priv].notify_pending, clock_priv_id)`.
   **No `ipc_to` check** (kernel-originated). Reuses the existing pub(crate)
   `will_receive` / `build_notify_message`.

5. **`kernel/src/system/do_setalarm.rs`** (new) — real handler replacing the
   stub. Caller-local signature `(caller: &mut Proc, _caller_priv: &Priv, msg: &mut Message) -> i32`.
   Read `delta: u64` from `payload[0..8]` (`from_ne_bytes`). Compute
   `prev_remaining = caller.alarm_at.saturating_sub(now)` (0 if disarmed). If
   `delta == 0` → disarm (`alarm_at = 0`); else `alarm_at = now + delta` and
   `clock::arm_alarm(alarm_at)`. Write `prev_remaining` into `payload[0..8]` (MINIX
   returns the previous time-left; RS ignores it). Return `OK`.

6. **`kernel/src/system/mod.rs`** — add `mod do_setalarm;`; change the
   `SYS_SETALARM` arm in `dispatch_caller_local` from `stubs::do_setalarm(...)` to
   `do_setalarm::do_setalarm(...)`. `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` stays **15** — the
   `const _: () = assert!(NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 == 15, …)` is unchanged (no new
   call number). Remove the `enosys_stub!(do_setalarm)` line from
   `kernel/src/system/stubs.rs`.

### Server

7. **`servers/rs/Cargo.toml`** (new) — mirror `servers/sched/Cargo.toml` (deps:
   `minixrs-kernel-shared`, `minixrs-ipc`, `minixrs-server-rt`; `publish = false`).

8. **`servers/rs/user.ld`** (new) — copy `servers/sched/user.ld` verbatim (base
   `0x0010_0000`, page-aligned PT_LOADs).

9. **`servers/rs/src/main.rs`** (new) — SEF loop per the protocol above
   (clone of `servers/sched/src/main.rs` structure). Monitored set =
   `[boot_endpoint(DS_PROC_NR), boot_endpoint(VM_PROC_NR), boot_endpoint(SCHED_PROC_NR), boot_endpoint(VFS_PROC_NR)]`.

10. **`servers/rs/src/monitor.rs`** (new) — pure liveness table + host tests
    (pattern: `servers/sched/src/policy.rs`).

### Build + boot

11. **`kernel/build.rs`** — append `("minixrs-rs", workspace.join("servers/rs"), 2)`
    to the `servers` array (length `4 → 5`; proc_nr 2 = `RS_PROC_NR`). Add
    `servers/rs/{src,user.ld,Cargo.toml}` to the rerun-if-changed list. No loader
    change — `userland.rs`'s `BootImage::iter()` loop + `init_boot_image` priv fill
    pick RS up automatically.

### Docs / config

12. **`docs/plan.md`** (as part of the PR): reconcile the **stale 4.3 marker**
    `◀ ready (branch feature/phase-4-3-sched, pending merge)` → `✓ shipped (PR #25,
    merged 2026-06-27)`; flip **4.4** `◀ next` → `◀ ready (branch
    feature/phase-4-4-rs-setalarm, pending merge)` with a shipped-style paragraph;
    slide `◀ next` to **4.5**.
13. **kernel-shared / sonar:** **no changes expected.** No new call numbers or
    RS request range (RS uses NOTIFY; `RS_RQ_BASE` deferred to 4.5 when PM drives
    RS). `servers/rs/src/main.rs` is auto-covered by the existing
    `servers/**/src/main.rs` sonar glob; `monitor.rs` is host-tested. Run the
    pre-PR `revise-claude-md` to capture any 4.4 conventions worth recording
    (alarm mechanism, RS shape).

---

## Critical files & reuse (don't reinvent)

- `servers/sched/src/{main.rs,policy.rs}`, `servers/sched/{Cargo.toml,user.ld}` —
  the server template RS clones.
- `kernel/src/ipc/notify.rs` — `will_receive`, `build_notify_message` (reuse for
  `deliver_alarm`); `take_pending_notification` in `receive.rs` already drains the
  deferred path → CLOCK endpoint.
- `kernel/src/ipc/mod.rs::{send_pagefault_to_vm, send_no_quantum}` — the
  kernel-originated-delivery wrapper pattern `fire_expired_alarms` follows.
- `kernel/src/clock.rs::{uptime, UPTIME}` — existing tick counter the alarm rides.
- `kernel/src/system/do_schedule.rs` (caller-local + payload accessors) /
  `stubs.rs` (`enosys_stub!`) — model for `do_setalarm`.
- `kernel/src/proc/table.rs` (`IMAGE` RS entry + `init_boot_image`) — confirms **no
  new priv wiring**; `userland.rs` load loop + `kernel/build.rs::pack_mxbi` —
  add RS by one `servers` row.

## Verification (QEMU is primary for kernel; host tests for pure logic)

```sh
cargo fmt --all && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test -p minixrs-rs -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-server-rt   # green
cargo kernel-aarch64
timeout 10 cargo run -p minixrs-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release \
  > /tmp/phase44.log 2>&1
grep -aE '\[as\]|\[alarm|\[ipc|\[ksys|panic|el0_sync_unexpected' /tmp/phase44.log
```

Expected in `/tmp/phase44.log`:
- **Nine `[as]` lines** — vm/ds/vfs/sched/**rs** servers + stubs A–D (RS is the new
  one; asids shift by one).
- RS boots through SEF: head `[ipc]` shows `caller=2` (RS) `GET_WHOAMI` + DS publish
  (DS replies RS).
- RS arms the alarm: a `[ksys]` `SYS_SETALARM` dispatch (`caller=2`, `call=10`,
  `result=0`) — may need the alarm trace below to be the reliable witness given
  `[ksys]` modulo sampling.
- **Periodic `[alarm N]` lines** for RS (~once/sec; the head carve-out shows the
  first few explicitly) — the core proof the timer fires.
- RS heartbeats: `[ipc]` `NOTIFY` ops `caller=2` → DS/VM/SCHED/VFS and ack
  `NOTIFY`s back (`caller=` each peer).
- **Regressions intact:** A↔B ping-pong, stub C `SYS_GETINFO` flood, stub D's
  three `[pf]` → VM `VMCTL_PT_MAP`/`_UNMAP`, SCHED `[noq]`/`SYS_SCHEDULE`
  round-trip. Every line `result=0`; **zero** `panic` / `el0_sync_unexpected`.

- Branch `feature/phase-4-4-rs-setalarm` off `main` (never commit to `main`).
- TDD `monitor.rs` (host tests first). Kernel alarm path verified in QEMU.
- Before PR: run `/claude-md-management:revise-claude-md`, then confirm with the
  user before opening the PR. Commits GPG-signed; no `--no-verify`/`--no-gpg-sign`.

content.text: Task 4 — create `do_setalarm.rs`. Let me check the `do_schedule.rs` helper/header style first.

content.content:
1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
2	// Copyright (c) 2025-2026 Kevin Barnard and minix.rs Contributors
3	//! `SYS_SCHEDULE` / `SYS_SCHEDCTL` — the kernel mechanism behind the
4	//! delegatable scheduler (slice 4.3).
5	//!
6	//! minix.rs keeps the priority-banded run queue in the kernel but makes it
7	//! *delegatable*: a user-space scheduler (SCHED) drives policy through these two
8	//! calls, exactly as VM drives paging policy through `SYS_VMCTL`. Both name a
9	//! *target* process by endpoint, so — like `do_vmctl` — they take the whole
10	//! `&mut [Proc]` slice + `caller_nr` and are routed specially in
11	//! [`kernel_call_dispatch`](super::kernel_call_dispatch).
12	//!
13	//! - [`do_schedule`] sets a target's priority + quantum and (re-)admits it to the
14	//!   run queue. SCHED issues it in response to `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM` (re-admit
15	//!   a preempted proc) and at `SCHEDULING_START` (initial assignment).
16	//! - [`do_schedctl`] claims a target (`target.scheduler = caller`) or releases it
17	//!   back to the kernel scheduler (`SCHEDCTL_FLAG_KERNEL` → `scheduler = NONE`).
18	//!
19	//! ## Trust model
20	//!
21	//! Identical to `do_vmctl`: the single gate is `Priv::k_call_mask` granting the
22	//! call, checked in `kernel_call_dispatch` before we run. A caller that reaches
23	//! here may name any process. SCHED is the intended sole holder; if a
24	//! less-trusted process is later granted these calls, per-target authorization
25	//! must be added first.
26	//!
27	//! ## Message payload layout (offsets within `Message::payload`)
28	//!
29	//! | offset | field            | direction | call          |
30	//! |--------|------------------|-----------|---------------|
31	//! |  0..4  | target endpoint  | in        | `SYS_SCHEDULE`|
32	//! |  4..8  | priority (i32)   | in        | `SYS_SCHEDULE`|

[package]
name = "minixrs-sched"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
license = "BSD-3-Clause"
publish = false

[[bin]]
name = "minixrs-sched"
path = "src/main.rs"

[dependencies]

minixrs-server-rt = { path = "../../server-rt" }

content.content:
1	/*
2	 * minix.rs user-space linker script (aarch64 EL0 servers).
3	 *
4	 * Produces a static ET_EXEC ELF that the kernel's minimal loader
5	 * (`kernel/src/boot_image/elf.rs`) maps segment-by-segment into a fresh
6	 * per-process AddrSpace. The loader is deliberately tiny, so this script
7	 * keeps every PT_LOAD page-aligned (vaddr *and* file offset) and emits no
8	 * dynamic sections / relocations.
9	 *
10	 * Load base 0x0010_0000 (1 MiB): comfortably below USER_VA_TOP and clear of
11	 * the kernel's hand-coded EL0 stub VAs (code 0x40_0000-0x43_0000, stacks
12	 * 0x80_0000-0x83_0000, stub-D heap 0x0100_0000). The stack is NOT part of
13	 * the ELF — the kernel allocates a stack frame and sets SP_EL0 before eret.
14	 * Each server gets its own per-process TTBR0, so this shared load base
15	 * never collides across servers.
16	 */
17	
18	OUTPUT_FORMAT(elf64-littleaarch64)
19	OUTPUT_ARCH(aarch64)
20	ENTRY(_start)
21	
22	PHDRS {
23	    text   PT_LOAD FLAGS(5); /* R+X */
24	    rodata PT_LOAD FLAGS(4); /* R   */
25	    data   PT_LOAD FLAGS(6); /* RW  */
26	}
27	
28	SECTIONS {
29	    . = 0x00100000;
30	
31	    .text : ALIGN(4K) {
32	        KEEP(*(.text._start))
33	        *(.text .text.*)
34	    } :text
35	
36	    . = ALIGN(4K);
37	    .rodata : ALIGN(4K) {
38	        *(.rodata .rodata.*)
39	    } :rodata
40	
41	    . = ALIGN(4K);
42	    .data : ALIGN(4K) {
43	        *(.data .data.*)
44	    } :data
45	
46	    .bss : ALIGN(4K) {
47	        *(.bss .bss.*)
48	        *(COMMON)
49	    } :data
50	
51	    /DISCARD/ : {
52	        *(.eh_frame)
53	        *(.eh_frame_hdr)
54	        *(.note .note.*)
55	        *(.comment)
56	        *(.dynsym .dynstr .hash .gnu.hash .dynamic)
57	    }
58	}
59

lines: // Copyright (c) 2025-2026 Kevin Barnard and minix.rs Contributors
+//! RS is the *root system process* — the parent/monitor of every system service
+//! (`ROOT_SYS_PROC`, and the `sig_mgr` for all). Slice 4.4 stands up its
+//! heartbeat loop: RS boots through SEF, publishes its endpoint to DS, arms a
+//! periodic kernel alarm via `SYS_SETALARM`, and on each alarm pings the boot
+//! servers and tallies which ones answered. Restart-on-crash is **minimal** in
+//! Phase 4 — RS *detects* an unresponsive peer (the `monitor` accounting) but,
+//! running at EL0 with no console and no exec yet, takes no live action; full
+//! reload via the boot archive is a later slice.
+//! ## Heartbeat protocol (all observable kernel-side; EL0 cannot print)
+//!   expiry). On each one RS sweeps the previous round, re-pings every peer, and
+//!   re-arms the one-shot alarm (so it is effectively periodic).
+//! - **ping** = `ipc_notify(peer)`. The peer's SEF runtime classifies a `NOTIFY`
+//!   from RS as a ping and acks with `ipc_notify(RS)`.
+//! - **ack** = a `NOTIFY` from a monitored peer. RS records it ([`monitor::mark_alive`]).
+//! Built as a freestanding aarch64 ELF (`servers/rs/user.ld`), packed into the
+//! boot-image archive by `kernel/build.rs`, and loaded into its `RS_PROC_NR`
+//! slot at boot. RS already has its priv slot fully wired by `init_boot_image`
+//! (`SRV_T` trap mask + `ipc_to` over all boot servers + `k_call_mask` covering
+//! `SYS_SETALARM`), so it needs no per-server priv code. The liveness accounting
+//! lives in [`monitor`], whose pure logic carries the host unit tests.
+// Freestanding for the bare-metal build, but a normal host binary under
+// `cargo test` so `monitor`'s logic gets host-runnable unit tests. Same gating
+    CLOCK, DS_PROC_NR, SCHED_PROC_NR, SYSTEM, VFS_PROC_NR, VM_PROC_NR, boot_endpoint,
+/// Boot servers RS heartbeats. Boot endpoints (generation 0) are valid for the
+/// whole kernel lifetime — boot procs never exit in Phase 4 — so a static list
+/// suffices; DS-driven discovery is a later enhancement.
+/// Heartbeat period, in clock ticks. At 100 Hz this is ≈1 s, giving ~8–10 fires
+/// in an 8 s QEMU run — frequent enough to observe, sparse enough not to flood.
+/// ELF entry point. The kernel primes `SP_EL0` before `eret`, so `_start` dives
+/// straight into Rust. Gated to `not(test)` (under `cargo test` the crate links
+/// as a host binary with the C runtime's `_start`); `.text._start` is gated to
+/// the bare-metal target so a Mach-O host `cargo check` still type-checks.
+    // `sef_startup` learns RS's endpoint/name via SYS_GETINFO(GET_WHOAMI) and
+    // runs `rs_init`, which publishes RS's endpoint to DS. No signal handling
+    // yet (PM drives signals from a later slice). On failure there is nothing to
+    // Begin monitoring: record the peers, send the first heartbeat round, and arm
+    // the first alarm. Pinging here means the first alarm's sweep evaluates real
+    // acks rather than a spurious all-missed round.
+        // RS's traffic is all NOTIFY: the alarm from CLOCK, and heartbeat acks
+        // from peers. The source is the discriminator.
+                // A heartbeat ack (or unrelated NOTIFY — mark_alive ignores it).
+        // Any other message: drop (RS has no request handlers in slice 4.4).
+/// SEF fresh-init callback: publish RS's endpoint to DS under its name so peers
+/// can look RS up. DS registers the caller's kernel-stamped endpoint.
+/// Alarm tick: close out the previous heartbeat round, re-ping every peer, and
+/// re-arm the one-shot alarm. The dead-peer count is detect-only in Phase 4 (RS
+/// cannot log from EL0 and has no restart path yet); the periodic fire itself is
+/// the observable proof, traced kernel-side as `[alarm N]`.
+/// Send one heartbeat (`NOTIFY`) to every monitored peer. Non-blocking; a peer
+/// that is not currently receiving gets the notification deferred and acks once
+/// Arm the caller's one-shot alarm `delta` ticks out via `SYS_SETALARM` (SENDREC
+/// to SYSTEM). The reply carries the previous timer's remaining ticks, which RS
+// The freestanding panic handler; under `cargo test` std supplies its own.

The 16 aarch64-clippy errors are **pre-existing** (e.g. `sched.rs:264` nomem-asm, `system/mod.rs:125` `n % KCALL_TRACE_EVERY == 0`) — they live in the `target_os="none"`-gated modules that the *host* clippy gate (which passed clean) never lints, and they ship on `main` today. My new code follows the existing `n % TRACE_EVERY == 0` convention, so I'm not fixing those out-of-scope lints. The kernel compiled cleanly via `cargo kernel-aarch64`, and the boot image now includes the `minixrs-rs` ELF.

content.text:
Decoding the head trace confirms the full RS lifecycle: `[ipc 3]` RS→SYSTEM (GET_WHOAMI), `[ipc 4]` RS→DS (publish), `[ipc 10]` DS→RS (publish reply), `[ipc 11/12]` RS→DS and RS→VM (heartbeat NOTIFYs). All `result=0`.

Only 3 alarm fires over a 10s run is suspicious — let me confirm whether the kernel simply didn't reach more ticks, or the alarm stopped re-arming. Let me check final uptime and RS's `SYS_SETALARM` re-arm calls.

content.text:
The kernel reached `[ipc 208600]` / `[ksys 208400]` but only 3 alarms — that's the **slow TCG guest**: alarms fire at exactly uptime 100/200/300, and the run only reached ~300 ticks of *guest* time in 10s wall-clock. Critically, **three consecutive periodic fires (100→200→300) prove RS re-arms correctly** — if re-arm were broken, alarm 1 would fire and then nothing. Regressions are intact: stub D's three faults→VM, SCHED `[noq]` delegation, all `result=0`, no panic.

  `alarm_at: u64` (absolute uptime tick, 0 = disarmed; `Proc::EMPTY` zeroes it),
  and `kernel/src/system/do_setalarm.rs` (caller-local, like the other
  non-target `SYS_*`) reads a relative `delta` (u64, payload `0..8`), stores
  `alarm_at = uptime()+delta` (disarms on `delta==0`), and replies the previous
  timer's remaining ticks. `clock.rs` gains an `EARLIEST_ALARM` fast-path gate
  (`arm_alarm`/`set_earliest_alarm`) so `tick()` stays O(1) and only pays the
  O(N) scan when an alarm is actually due; the scan + delivery live in
  `ipc::fire_expired_alarms` (a kernel-originated-delivery wrapper beside
  `send_pagefault_to_vm`/`send_no_quantum`), which clears each expired
  `alarm_at`, calls `ipc::notify::deliver_alarm`, traces `[alarm N]` (head
  carve-out + modulo, like `TRACE_HEAD`), and recomputes the next-earliest.
  `deliver_alarm` is a kernel-originated `NOTIFY` from `CLOCK` with **no `ipc_to`
  check** (CLOCK's bitmap is empty, so routing through `mini_notify` would deny
  it) — immediate when the owner is `RECEIVE`-blocked, else deferred via
  `notify_pending` against CLOCK's priv slot (drained by `mini_receive`). No new
  kernel-shared constants — the alarm reuses `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` + `CLOCK`, and
  `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` stays 15. RS (`servers/rs`, server-rt based, already
  `ROOT_SYS_PROC` + `sig_mgr` with full priv wiring from `init_boot_image`) boots
  through SEF, publishes to DS, arms a periodic alarm (`ALARM_PERIOD = 100`
  ticks), and on each fire pings a static peer set (DS/VM/SCHED/VFS via
  `boot_endpoint`) with `ipc_notify`, tallying acks in a host-tested
  `servers/rs/src/monitor.rs` (`UnsafeCell` newtype like `sched/policy.rs`);
  restart-on-crash is detect-only (the `monitor::sweep` dead count — EL0 can't
  log and exec is a later slice). The RS heartbeat reuses the existing SEF ping
  (peers ack via `server-rt`'s `sef.receive`); the alarm `NOTIFY` from CLOCK is
  classified `Application` (source ≠ RS), so RS's loop keys on
  `m_source == boot_endpoint(CLOCK)`. Added to the MXBI `servers` array in
  `build.rs` (proc_nr 2); no new boot priv wiring. Verified in QEMU over 25 s:
  nine `[as]` lines (vm/ds/vfs/sched/rs asid 1–5, stubs A–D asid 6–9); head
  `[ipc]` shows RS GET_WHOAMI + DS publish (`[ipc 3/4]`), DS reply to RS
  (`[ipc 10]`), and RS's first heartbeat pings to DS/VM (`[ipc 11/12]`); six
  periodic `[alarm N] owner=r nr=2 at=100..600` fires; stub D's three `[pf]` →
  VM, SCHED `[noq]` delegation, and A↔B ping-pong + C `SYS_GETINFO` all intact;
  every line `result=0`; zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`. Host: `cargo test
  -p minixrs-rs -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-server-rt` green; `cargo
  check --workspace` + clippy `-D warnings` + fmt clean.

904	- **Slice 4.4** ◀ next — RS (reincarnation server) + real `SYS_SETALARM`. Implement the
905	  per-proc one-shot alarm off the clock tick (currently ENOSYS). RS (already
906	  `ROOT_SYS_PROC` + `sig_mgr`) records the boot servers, arms a periodic alarm,
907	  and heartbeats/pings them on each tick; restart-on-crash is minimal (detect +
908	  log) in Phase 4.
909	- **Slice 4.5** — PM part A: process table + getpid + `SYS_PRIVCTL` + minimal
910	  signals. Stand up PM with a static `mproc` table, `getpid`/`getppid`, and a
911	  kill path whose default action is terminate (no handlers/sigaction/masks —
912	  that's beyond Phase 4). Make `SYS_PRIVCTL` real (set up a target's priv slot;
913	  the 4.6 fork path leans on it). This gives the Phase-3 VM "SIGSEGV leaves
914	  faulter blocked" path a real consequence (PM terminates the faulter).
915	- **Slice 4.6** — PM part B: fork + exit + wait. Kernel `SYS_FORK` (free slot,
916	  copy register frame, bump generation, alloc ASID, eager AS copy by walking the
917	  parent's TTBR0 + copying each user page via HHDM; CoW deferred) and `SYS_EXIT`
918	  (tear down AS via `AddrSpace::destroy`, free slot). Forked user procs share one
919	  USER priv slot (MINIX's `static_priv` model) so fork can't exhaust the 64-slot
920	  `PRIV_TABLE`. `VM_FORK` clones the parent's `ClientRegions` into the child. PM
921	  owns the tree: fork → `SYS_FORK` + `VM_FORK` + `SCHEDULING_START`; exit →
922	  zombie + SIGCHLD; `wait`/`waitpid` → reap.
923	- **Slice 4.7** — exec: `SYS_EXEC` + PM exec of a boot-embedded binary. Kernel
924	  builds a fresh `AddrSpace`, `elf::load_into` the archive module resolved by
925	  `module_by_name`, sets a minimal initial user stack, swaps `ttbr0_pa` +
926	  destroys the old AS, sets entry/SP. PM's `execve(name)` selects the embedded
927	  binary. A tiny freestanding "worker" ELF packed into the archive is the exec
928	  target.
929	- **Slice 4.8** — init (PID 1) + Phase 4 wrap-up + docs. **Phase 4 complete.**

file.content:
- **Slice 4.4** ◀ next — RS (reincarnation server) + real `SYS_SETALARM`. Implement the
  per-proc one-shot alarm off the clock tick (currently ENOSYS). RS (already
  `ROOT_SYS_PROC` + `sig_mgr`) records the boot servers, arms a periodic alarm,
  and heartbeats/pings them on each tick; restart-on-crash is minimal (detect +
  log) in Phase 4.
- **Slice 4.5** — PM part A: process table + getpid + `SYS_PRIVCTL` + minimal
  signals. Stand up PM with a static `mproc` table, `getpid`/`getppid`, and a
  kill path whose default action is terminate (no handlers/sigaction/masks —
  that's beyond Phase 4). Make `SYS_PRIVCTL` real (set up a target's priv slot;
  the 4.6 fork path leans on it). This gives the Phase-3 VM "SIGSEGV leaves
  faulter blocked" path a real consequence (PM terminates the faulter).
- **Slice 4.6** — PM part B: fork + exit + wait. Kernel `SYS_FORK` (free slot,
  copy register frame, bump generation, alloc ASID, eager AS copy by walking the
  parent's TTBR0 + copying each user page via HHDM; CoW deferred) and `SYS_EXIT`
  (tear down AS via `AddrSpace::destroy`, free slot). Forked user procs share one
  USER priv slot (MINIX's `static_priv` model) so fork can't exhaust the 64-slot
  `PRIV_TABLE`. `VM_FORK` clones the parent's `ClientRegions` into the child. PM
  owns the tree: fork → `SYS_FORK` + `VM_FORK` + `SCHEDULING_START`; exit →
  zombie + SIGCHLD; `wait`/`waitpid` → reap.
- **Slice 4.7** — exec: `SYS_EXEC` + PM exec of a boot-embedded binary. Kernel
  builds a fresh `AddrSpace`, `elf::load_into` the archive module resolved by
  `module_by_name`, sets a minimal initial user stack, swaps `ttbr0_pa` +
  destroys the old AS, sets entry/SP. PM's `execve(name)` selects the embedded
  binary. A tiny freestanding "worker" ELF packed into the archive is the exec
  target.
- **Slice 4.8** — init (PID 1) + Phase 4 wrap-up + docs. **Phase 4 complete.**

  non-target `SYS_*`) reads a relative `delta` (u64, payload `0..8`), stores
  `alarm_at = uptime()+delta` (disarms on `delta==0`), and replies the previous
  timer's remaining ticks. `clock.rs` gains an `EARLIEST_ALARM` fast-path gate
  (`arm_alarm`/`set_earliest_alarm`) so `tick()` stays O(1) and only pays the
  O(N) scan when an alarm is actually due; the scan + delivery live in
  `ipc::fire_expired_alarms` (a kernel-originated-delivery wrapper beside
  `send_pagefault_to_vm`/`send_no_quantum`), which clears each expired
  `alarm_at`, calls `ipc::notify::deliver_alarm`, traces `[alarm N]` (head
  carve-out + modulo, like `TRACE_HEAD`), and recomputes the next-earliest.
  `deliver_alarm` is a kernel-originated `NOTIFY` from `CLOCK` with **no `ipc_to`
  check** (CLOCK's bitmap is empty, so routing through `mini_notify` would deny
  it) — immediate when the owner is `RECEIVE`-blocked, else deferred via
  `notify_pending` against CLOCK's priv slot (drained by `mini_receive`). No new
  kernel-shared constants — the alarm reuses `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` + `CLOCK`, and
  `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` stays 15. RS (`servers/rs`, server-rt based, already

content.text:
Review this session for learnings about working with Claude Code in this codebase. Update CLAUDE.md with context that would help future Claude sessions be more effective.

## Step 1: Reflect

What context was missing that would have helped Claude work more effectively?
- Bash commands that were used or discovered
- Code style patterns followed
- Testing approaches that worked
- Environment/configuration quirks
- Warnings or gotchas encountered

## Step 2: Find CLAUDE.md Files

```bash
find . -name "CLAUDE.md" -o -name ".claude.local.md" 2>/dev/null | head -20
```

Decide where each addition belongs:

content.text:
Reflecting on this session, here are the highest-value, likely-to-recur learnings worth capturing. The project CLAUDE.md is already detailed, so I'll keep these tight and let you choose.

### Proposed additions to `./CLAUDE.md`

**1. CI gotcha — host clippy doesn't lint kernel code** (CI section)

**Why:** I ran `cargo clippy -p minixrs-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none` and hit 16 errors that turned out to be pre-existing and *not* CI-gated — a future session could waste time "fixing" them or be confused why CI is green despite them.

```diff
+ - The blocking `clippy --workspace` gate runs on the **host** target, where the kernel's
+   real modules are `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]`-gated out — so kernel code is *not* clippy-linted
+   by CI. `cargo clippy -p minixrs-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none` surfaces those lints, but
+   it currently reports pre-existing ones that ship on `main` (nomem-asm pointers, `manual_is_multiple_of`,
+   interior-mutable-const); don't "fix" them as part of an unrelated slice. `cargo kernel-aarch64` is the real compile gate for kernel code.
```

**2. TCG slow-guest / uptime for time-based verification** (Build section)

**Why:** A 10 s `timeout` run only reached ~300 ticks of guest time (3 alarm fires); I had to reason carefully that this was the slow TCG guest, not a bug, and re-run at 25 s to show sustained periodicity.

```diff
+ # QEMU under TCG advances *guest* time slower than wall-clock, so a `timeout N` run reaches far
+ # fewer than N×100 ticks. For time-based features (alarms, quantum/scheduling) read uptime-stamped
+ # traces (e.g. `[alarm … at=N]`) as the real clock, and run 20-25 s to observe several periods.
```

**3. Per-proc alarm mechanism** (Code Conventions bullet list)

**Why:** New kernel subsystem (slice 4.4) that PM's `alarm()` and SCHED aging will reuse; matches the style of the existing per-slice convention bullets.

```diff
+ - Per-proc one-shot alarms (slice 4.4): `Proc::alarm_at` holds an absolute uptime tick (0 = disarmed);
+   `clock::EARLIEST_ALARM` is an O(1) fast-path gate so `tick()` only pays the O(N) scan in
+   `ipc::fire_expired_alarms` when one is due. Expiry delivers a kernel-originated `NOTIFY` from `CLOCK`
+   via `ipc::notify::deliver_alarm` — **no `ipc_to` check** (kernel-originated, like `mini_pf_send`;
+   `CLOCK`'s `ipc_to` is empty so `mini_notify` would deny it). A periodic alarm is a re-arm per fire
+   (RS). `SYS_SETALARM` payload: relative `delta` ticks in `0..8` (0 cancels), reply = previous time-left.
```

Want me to apply all three, a subset, or none? (Bigger CLAUDE.md/docs prose lands in the slice-4.8 wrap-up regardless.)

# Build kernel for x86_64

# Run host-side unit tests (note the package name, not the dir name)
cargo test -p minixrs-kernel-shared
```

## CI

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs on every PR and on pushes to `main`. Seven gates run in
parallel — `fmt`, `clippy`, `audit` (cargo-audit), `deny` (cargo-deny, config in `deny.toml`),
`geiger`, `miri`, `coverage` (cargo-llvm-cov → `lcov.info`) — then a `sonar` job feeds the LCOV
report to SonarQube Cloud (org `minixrs`, project `minixrs_minixrs`, config in
`sonar-project.properties`). The Sonar scan auto-detects PR vs branch: PRs get decoration, `main`
pushes refresh the whole-project picture.

- `geiger` and `miri` are **advisory** (`continue-on-error`); the rest block. miri only covers the
  two host-testable crates (`-p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-vm`) — `minix-ipc` has inline asm
- Before pushing, the blocking gates must be green: `cargo fmt --all --check` and
  `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`. Run `cargo fmt --all` to fix formatting
- The toolchain is **pinned to a dated nightly** in `rust-toolchain.toml` (bare `nightly` let new
  lints/fmt rules break CI with no code change); bump it deliberately, not incidentally
- `Cargo.lock` **is committed** (so audit/deny are reproducible) — do not re-add it to `.gitignore`
- Third-party actions are pinned to full commit SHAs with `# vN` comments; keep that when editing
- SonarCloud needs the `SONAR_TOKEN` repo secret and Automatic Analysis disabled (CI-based instead)
- **Publishing:** `.github/workflows/release.yml` runs on a `v*` tag push and `cargo publish`es the
  five library crates to crates.io in dependency order (`minixrs-kernel-shared` → `minixrs-ipc` →
  `minixrs-server-rt` → `minixrs-driver-rt` → `minixrs` facade). All other members carry
  `publish = false` (freestanding binaries, unbuildable on registry infra). Needs the
  `CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN` repo secret. Bottom-up order is mandatory — crates.io forbids `path`-only
  deps, so the libs' path deps carry an explicit `version`. Verify locally with
  `cargo package -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-ipc -p minixrs-server-rt -p minixrs-driver-rt
  -p minixrs` (verify-builds against packaged siblings) — `cargo publish --dry-run` resolves deps
  against the registry so it can't chain, and `cargo package --workspace` aborts on the
  `publish = false` binaries. See `RELEASING.md`

## Architecture

See `docs/architecture.md` for the full system design. Key concepts:

- **Microkernel:** Only IPC, scheduling, interrupt dispatch, and memory protection in kernel
- **Message passing:** 6 IPC primitives (SEND, RECEIVE, SENDREC, NOTIFY, SENDNB, SENDA)
- **User-space servers:** All OS services (file system, process management, memory management) run as separate processes communicating via IPC
- **Privilege model:** Fine-grained bitmaps control which processes can communicate and what kernel calls they can make

## Workspace Crates

| Crate | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `kernel` | Microkernel (no_std) |
| `kernel-shared` | Message types, endpoints, call numbers shared between kernel and userspace |
| `minix-ipc` | User-space IPC library (SVC/SYSCALL asm stubs) |
| `server-rt` | Server runtime / SEF framework |
| `servers/*` | System servers (PM, VFS, VM, RS, DS, SCHED) |
| `drivers/*` | Device drivers (VirtIO block/net/console, memory) |
| `fs/*` | File system servers (MFS, PFS) |
| `userland/*` | User programs (init, sh, coreutils) |

## Code Conventions

- Every new `.rs`/`.S` source file must begin with the SPDX + copyright header before any other content. Rust (line-comment form): `// SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause` then `// Copyright (c) 2025-2026 Kevin Barnard and minix.rs Contributors`. Assembly `.S` (block-comment form): `/* SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause */` then `/* Copyright (c) 2025-2026 Kevin Barnard and minix.rs Contributors */`. Update the year as needed; `.toml`/`.ld`/`.conf` files get no header
- The kernel ships `--release` only, so `debug_assert!` is compiled out. Use a hard `assert!` for invariants whose violation would silently corrupt (e.g. a null TTBR0/ASID reaching the scheduler); reserve `debug_assert!` for cheap "can't happen" documentation that's fine to drop in release
- Kernel `unsafe` blocks require `// SAFETY:` comments documenting the invariant
- IPC linked lists use `Option<ProcNr>` indices into static arrays, not raw pointers

content.content: 130:- The kernel scheduler is **delegatable** (slice 4.3): `Proc::scheduler == NONE` (the boot default, set by `populate_proc`) means kernel-scheduled — `sched::reschedule` refills the quantum and rotates as before. A non-`NONE` `scheduler` endpoint means SCHED-scheduled: on quantum exhaustion `reschedule` dequeues the proc, leaves `RTS_NO_QUANTUM` set, and sends `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM` to the scheduler via `ipc::send::mini_sched_no_quantum_send` (a `mini_pf_send` clone; wrapper `ipc::send_no_quantum` materializes the proc-table slice like `send_pagefault_to_vm`). The proc stays off the run queue until the scheduler calls real `SYS_SCHEDULE` (`do_schedule` sets priority/quantum + `rts_unset(RTS_NO_QUANTUM)`). Kernel tasks **and SCHED itself stay `NONE`** — a scheduler must not schedule itself. `SYS_SCHEDCTL` claims (`scheduler = caller`) / releases (`SCHEDCTL_FLAG_KERNEL` → `NONE`) a target; the kernel pre-delegates stub C in `userland.rs` as the live demo until PM/RS drive `SCHEDULING_START` (4.5/4.6). SCHED's `SCHEDULING_*` request range is `SCHED_RQ_BASE = 0xF00` (clear of VM `0xC00` / SEF `0xD00` / DS `0xE00`, below `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`)

RS (`servers/rs`) heartbeats peers by `ipc_notify` (peers ack via `server-rt`'s SEF ping); RS keys its alarm on `m_source == boot_endpoint(CLOCK)` (classified `Application`, not the RS-sourced SEF ping)

lines: - IPC blocking pairs with the new `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` helpers — they capture `nr`, end the `&mut Proc` borrow, then call `enqueue` / `dequeue` so RTS state and the run queue stay in sync. Same NLL-capture pattern slice 2.4 used in `clock::tick`

- Kernel-call handlers that act on a *target* proc named in the message (e.g. `system::do_vmctl`, `system::do_schedule`'s `do_schedule`/`do_schedctl`) take the whole `&mut [Proc; N_PROC_SLOTS]` slice + `caller_nr`; caller-only handlers (e.g. `do_getinfo`) get a single `&mut Proc` / `&Priv`. `system::kernel_call_dispatch` routes `SYS_VMCTL` / `SYS_SCHEDULE` / `SYS_SCHEDCTL` to the table-taking form (a small `match` before `dispatch_caller_local`) and the rest through `dispatch_caller_local`. Run-queue transitions on a target use the same `sched::rts_set` / `rts_unset` capture-then-borrow-end pattern the IPC primitives use

- The kernel scheduler is **delegatable** (slice 4.3): `Proc::scheduler == NONE` (the boot default, set by `populate_proc`) means kernel-scheduled — `sched::reschedule` refills the quantum and rotates as before. A non-`NONE` `scheduler` endpoint means SCHED-scheduled: on quantum exhaustion `reschedule` dequeues the proc, leaves `RTS_NO_QUANTUM` set, and sends `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM` to the scheduler via `ipc::send::mini_sched_no_quantum_send` (a `mini_pf_send` clone; wrapper `ipc::send_no_quantum` materializes the proc-table slice like `send_pagefault_to_vm`). The proc stays off the run queue until the scheduler calls real `SYS_SCHEDULE` (`do_schedule` sets priority/quantum + `rts_unset(RTS_NO_QUANTUM)`). Kernel tasks **and SCHED itself stay `NONE`** — a scheduler must not schedule itself. `SYS_SCHEDCTL` claims (`scheduler = caller`) / releases (`SCHEDCTL_FLAG_KERNEL` → `NONE`) a target; the kernel pre-delegates stub C in `userland.rs` as the live demo until PM/RS drive `SCHEDULING_START` (4.5/4.6). SCHED's `SCHEDULING_*` request range is `SCHED_RQ_BASE = 0xF00` (clear of VM `0xC00` / SEF `0xD00` / DS `0xE00`, below `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`)

+- Per-proc one-shot alarms (slice 4.4): `Proc::alarm_at` holds an absolute uptime tick (0 = disarmed); `clock::EARLIEST_ALARM` is an O(1) fast-path gate so `tick()` only pays the O(N) scan in `ipc::fire_expired_alarms` when one is due. Expiry delivers a kernel-originated `NOTIFY` from `CLOCK` via `ipc::notify::deliver_alarm` — **no `ipc_to` check** (kernel-originated, like `mini_pf_send`; `CLOCK`'s `ipc_to` is empty so `mini_notify` would deny it), immediate if the owner is `RECEIVE`-blocked else deferred via `notify_pending` against CLOCK's priv slot. A user-space periodic alarm is a re-arm per fire (RS). `SYS_SETALARM` (now real, caller-local) payload: relative `delta` ticks in `0..8` (0 cancels), reply = previous time-left; no new kernel-shared constants (reuses `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` + `CLOCK`). RS (`servers/rs`) heartbeats peers by `ipc_notify` (peers ack via `server-rt`'s SEF ping); RS keys its alarm on `m_source == boot_endpoint(CLOCK)` (classified `Application`, not the RS-sourced SEF ping)

- `kernel/build.rs` skips assembly when `CARGO_CFG_TARGET_OS != "none"` so `cargo check --workspace` / `cargo test --workspace` keep working on host. The kernel's real modules are gated by `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` in `main.rs` regardless

- `cargo test -p minixrs-kernel` runs zero tests by design — every kernel module is gated on `#[cfg(target_os = "none")]` and host-test infra is not yet built; host-runnable tests live in `kernel-shared`. QEMU is the primary verification for kernel code (`timeout 8 cargo run -p minixrs-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release`)

- `no_std` library crates that host-test via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), no_std)]` get linted in their std test-config too (`clippy --all-targets`): a const-only `assert!(A > B)` trips `assertions_on_constants` (use a module-level `const _: () = assert!(…)` like `callnr.rs`), and a bare `loop {}` in a function present under `test` trips `empty_loop` (use `loop { core::hint::spin_loop() }`; the `#[cfg(not(test))]` panic handler's `loop {}` is exempt because it's absent under test)

content.text:
Slice 4.4 is **implemented and verified** on branch `feature/phase-4-4-rs-setalarm`. Here's where things stand.

## What landed

**Kernel — real `SYS_SETALARM`:**
- `Proc::alarm_at` (absolute uptime tick, 0 = disarmed)
- `clock.rs`: `EARLIEST_ALARM` O(1) fast-path gate + `arm_alarm`/`set_earliest_alarm`; `tick()` only scans when an alarm is due
- `ipc::fire_expired_alarms` (kernel-originated-delivery wrapper) + `ipc::notify::deliver_alarm` (NOTIFY from `CLOCK`, no `ipc_to` check, immediate-or-deferred)
- `system/do_setalarm.rs` replacing the `ENOSYS` stub; `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` stays 15, **no new kernel-shared constants**

**RS server (`servers/rs`):** SEF loop, publishes to DS, arms a 100-tick periodic alarm, heartbeats DS/VM/SCHED/VFS via `ipc_notify`, liveness tallied in host-tested `monitor.rs` (8/8 tests). Added to the MXBI `servers` array — no new priv wiring.

## Verification (QEMU)
- 9 `[as]` lines incl. `rs nr=2 asid=5`; RS GET_WHOAMI + DS publish + DS reply + first heartbeat pings all visible in the `[ipc]` head
- **6 periodic alarm fires** at `at=100…600` — proves arm → fire → NOTIFY → re-arm is sustained
- Regressions intact: stub D's 3 faults→VM, SCHED `[noq]` delegation, A↔B + C — every line `result=0`, zero panics
- `fmt` clean, `clippy --workspace -D warnings` clean, host tests green, `cargo kernel-aarch64` clean

`docs/plan.md` markers reconciled (4.3 → shipped PR #25; 4.4 → ready; next → 4.5), and CLAUDE.md got the 3 learnings you approved.

## Not yet done (awaiting your go-ahead)
Per your workflow, I've **not committed**. When you're ready I'll commit the change set, push the branch, and open the PR. Want me to proceed — and any extra context/notes you'd like in the PR body?

lastPrompt: show me the logs

content.text:
The logs confirm the whole slice end-to-end. Reading the head trace as RS's lifecycle:

- **`[ipc 3]`** RS → SYSTEM (`SENDREC`, GET_WHOAMI) → **`[ipc 4]`** RS → DS (publish) → **`[ipc 10]`** DS → RS (`SEND`, publish reply) — RS boots through SEF and registers.
- **`[ipc 11/12]`** RS → DS and RS → VM (`call=4` NOTIFY) — first heartbeat round. (Pings to SCHED/VFS fall past the 12-line head carve-out.)
- **`[alarm 1…6]` at `at=100,200,…,600`** — the timer fires, RS wakes, re-arms, repeat. Six evenly-spaced fires is the proof that arm→fire→NOTIFY→re-arm is sustained, not a one-shot fluke.

Regressions are all green in the same boot:
- Stub D's three faults (`far=0x1000000`, `0x1004000`, `0x2000000` = brk×2 + mmap) → VM resolves each with `VMCTL_PT_MAP`, plus the one `VMCTL_PT_UNMAP` for the never-touched mmap page (the slice 3.6 pattern, untouched).
- SCHED no-quantum delegation (`[noq] proc=C → scheduler=0x9`) ×6.
- **0** panic/`el0_sync_unexpected` lines, **0** nonzero `result=` across ~9K `[ipc]` + ~9K `[ksys]` ops.

The only behavioral note worth knowing: 6 alarms (not the ~10/run I'd guessed) because QEMU's TCG guest clock runs slower than wall-clock — uptime only reached ~600 ticks in 25 s. That's the slow-guest reality I captured in the CLAUDE.md build note, not a defect; the `at=` stamps are the real clock.

Want me to commit + push + open the PR now?

input.content:
phase 4.4: RS reincarnation server + real SYS_SETALARM

Implement SYS_SETALARM as a per-proc one-shot timer hung off the clock
tick, and stand up the RS server that boots through SEF, publishes to DS,
and heartbeats the boot servers on a periodic alarm.

Kernel:
- Proc::alarm_at (absolute uptime tick, 0 = disarmed)
- clock::EARLIEST_ALARM O(1) fast-path gate so tick() only scans when due
- ipc::fire_expired_alarms (scan + recompute) and ipc::notify::deliver_alarm
  (kernel-originated NOTIFY from CLOCK, no ipc_to check, immediate-or-deferred)
- system/do_setalarm.rs replaces the ENOSYS stub (caller-local; relative
  delta in payload 0..8, reply = previous time-left)
- no new kernel-shared constants (reuses NOTIFY_MESSAGE + CLOCK);
  NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 stays 15

SYS_SETALARM, ipc_notify heartbeat to DS/VM/SCHED/VFS, host-tested

## Slice 4.4 — RS (reincarnation server) + real `SYS_SETALARM`

Next slice of `docs/plan.md` (Phase 4). Adds the kernel alarm mechanism and stands up RS, the next server in the DS→RS→SCHED→VFS→PM→init boot chain.

### Kernel — real `SYS_SETALARM` (was an `ENOSYS` stub)
- `Proc::alarm_at` — absolute uptime tick the one-shot timer fires at (0 = disarmed; `Proc::EMPTY` zeroes it).
- `clock.rs` — `EARLIEST_ALARM` O(1) fast-path gate (`arm_alarm`/`set_earliest_alarm`); `tick()` only pays the O(N) scan when an alarm is actually due.
- `ipc::fire_expired_alarms` — kernel-originated-delivery wrapper (beside `send_pagefault_to_vm`/`send_no_quantum`): clears each expired `alarm_at`, delivers, traces `[alarm N]` (head carve-out + modulo), recomputes the next-earliest.
- `ipc::notify::deliver_alarm` — kernel-originated `NOTIFY` from `CLOCK`, **no `ipc_to` check** (CLOCK's bitmap is empty, so `mini_notify` would deny it); immediate when the owner is `RECEIVE`-blocked, else deferred via `notify_pending` (drained by `mini_receive`).
- `system/do_setalarm.rs` — caller-local handler; relative `delta` ticks in payload `0..8` (0 cancels), reply = previous time-left.
- **No new kernel-shared constants** (reuses `NOTIFY_MESSAGE` + `CLOCK`); `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4` stays 15.

### RS server (`servers/rs`)
- server-rt SEF loop; publishes to DS at init; arms a periodic alarm (`ALARM_PERIOD = 100` ticks); on each fire pings a static peer set (DS/VM/SCHED/VFS via `boot_endpoint`) with `ipc_notify` and tallies acks.
- Liveness accounting in host-tested `monitor.rs` (`UnsafeCell` newtype like `sched/policy.rs`); restart-on-crash is **detect-only** in Phase 4 (EL0 can't log, exec is a later slice).
- RS heartbeat reuses the existing SEF ping (peers ack via `server-rt`); the alarm `NOTIFY` from CLOCK classifies `Application` (source ≠ RS), so RS keys on `m_source == boot_endpoint(CLOCK)`.
- Added to the MXBI `servers` array in `build.rs` (proc_nr 2). **No new boot priv wiring** — `init_boot_image` already grants RS `SRV_T` `ipc_to` + `k_call_mask`.

### Verification (QEMU, 25 s)
- Nine `[as]` lines: vm/ds/vfs/sched/**rs** (asid 1–5) + stubs A–D (asid 6–9).
- `[ipc]` head: RS GET_WHOAMI (`[ipc 3]`) + DS publish (`[ipc 4]`) + DS reply to RS (`[ipc 10]`) + heartbeat pings to DS/VM (`[ipc 11/12]`).
- **Six periodic `[alarm N] owner=r nr=2 at=100..600` fires** — proves arm → fire → NOTIFY → re-arm is sustained.
- Regressions intact: stub D's three faults → VM (`VMCTL_PT_MAP` ×3 + `VMCTL_PT_UNMAP`), SCHED `[noq]` delegation ×6, A↔B + C `SYS_GETINFO`.
- Every line `result=0`; **zero** panic / `el0_sync_unexpected` across ~9K `[ipc]` + ~9K `[ksys]`.
- Host: `cargo test -p minixrs-rs -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-server-rt` green (monitor 8/8); `cargo fmt --all --check` + `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo kernel-aarch64` clean.

> Note: 6 (not ~10) alarm fires because QEMU's TCG guest clock runs slower than wall-clock — uptime reached ~600 ticks in 25 s. The `at=` stamps are the real clock; captured as a CLAUDE.md build note.

### Docs
- `docs/plan.md`: reconciled stale 4.3 marker → `✓ shipped (PR #25, merged 2026-06-27)`; 4.4 → `◀ ready`; `◀ next` → 4.5.
- `CLAUDE.md`: alarm-mechanism convention, host-clippy-doesn't-lint-kernel gotcha, TCG slow-guest verification note.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
gh pr create --base main --head feature/phase-4-4-rs-setalarm \
  --title "phase 4.4: RS reincarnation server + real SYS_SETALARM" \

## Summary

- **Commit** `b3e4fae` — `phase 4.4: RS reincarnation server + real SYS_SETALARM` (GPG-signed, verified `Good signature`).
- **Branch** `feature/phase-4-4-rs-setalarm` pushed to `origin`.
- **PR #26** → https://github.com/minixrs/minixrs/pull/26 (base `main`).

The PR body carries the full kernel/server breakdown, the QEMU verification (9 `[as]` lines, 6 periodic `[alarm]` fires, regressions intact, all `result=0`, no panics), the TCG slow-guest note, and the `docs/plan.md` + `CLAUDE.md` updates.

CI's seven gates (`fmt`, `clippy`, `audit`, `deny`, `geiger`, `miri`, `coverage` → `sonar`) will run on the PR now. Want me to keep an eye on the checks and report back, or is there anything else for this slice?
